1292	# Codex round 1 B-1: content-word overlap check for non-numeric claims.
1293	# Stopwords are the "grammatical connective tissue" — if the only overlap
1294	# between a sentence and its cited span is "the" and "of", that's not
1295	# grounding. We strip stopwords and check for overlap of real content words.
1296	_STOPWORDS_FOR_GROUNDING = frozenset({
1297	    "a", "an", "and", "are", "as", "at", "be", "but", "by", "can", "could",
1298	    "did", "do", "does", "doing", "for", "from", "had", "has", "have",
1299	    "having", "he", "her", "here", "him", "his", "how", "i", "if", "in",
1300	    "into", "is", "it", "its", "itself", "may", "me", "might", "my", "no",
1301	    "not", "of", "on", "or", "our", "out", "over", "own", "same", "she",
1302	    "should", "so", "some", "such", "than", "that", "the", "their", "them",
1303	    "then", "there", "these", "they", "this", "those", "through", "to",
1304	    "too", "under", "up", "very", "was", "we", "were", "what", "when",
1305	    "where", "which", "while", "who", "whom", "why", "will", "with",
1306	    "would", "you", "your", "yours",
1307	    # filler / weak verbs
1308	    "been", "being", "also", "more", "most", "other", "any", "all",
1309	    "each", "both", "only", "just", "even", "new", "old", "one", "two",
1310	    "three", "four", "five",
1311	})
1312	
1313	
1314	def _content_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
1315	    """Extract lowercased content words (alphabetic, length >=3) minus
1316	    stopwords. Used by the B-1 semantic-grounding check.
1317	
1318	    I-deepfix-001 L4 (#1344): the Latin path below is byte-identical to the
1319	    pre-L4 engine (English unchanged). It is UNIONED with script-aware tokens
1320	    (CJK character bigrams + space-delimited non-Latin words) so the overlap
1321	    floor counts correctly on non-Latin claims instead of silently returning
1322	    the empty set. Reverts byte-identical under PG_STRICT_VERIFY_SCRIPT_AWARE=0.
1323	    """
1324	    if not text:
1325	        return set()
1326	    # Find alphabetic tokens, ignore numbers and punctuation
1327	    tokens = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z\-]{2,}", text.lower())
1328	    latin = {t for t in tokens if t not in _STOPWORDS_FOR_GROUNDING}
1329	    return latin | extra_script_tokens(text)
1330	
1331	
1332	# Minimum content-word overlap between a sentence and any of its cited
1333	# spans. Default is 2 (Codex round 2 finding): overlap=1 is exploitable
1334	# because a fabricated predicate sharing one anchor noun with the source
1335	# (e.g., "Aspirin reduced pain" cited to "Aspirin caused bleeding") would
1336	# verify with nothing but "aspirin" in common. The operator can lower
1337	# via PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP=1 for legitimate short sentences,
1338	# but the default must enforce a real semantic floor.
1339	MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP = int(
1340	    os.getenv("PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP", "2")
1341	)
1342	
1343	# I-complete-003 (#1189) — PROVENANCE RE-ANCHOR.
1344	#
1345	# When a findings sentence FAILS verification on its CURRENTLY-cited span,
1346	# before the sentence is dropped the re-anchor enumerates a BOUNDED set of
1347	# candidate spans WITHIN the SAME cited evidence row (or, for an UNCITED but
1348	# verbatim-grounded sentence, the pool row that verbatim-contains it) and
1349	# re-runs the EXACT same `verify_sentence_provenance` acceptance gate against
1350	# each candidate. The FIRST candidate that passes the FULL gate (numeric +
1351	# >=MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP content overlap + trial-name + NLI entailment)
1352	# re-binds the sentence's [#ev:...] token to that span and the sentence is
1353	# kept as RECOVERED. If NO candidate passes, the original drop stands — there
1354	# is NO new acceptance path, so the re-anchor can ONLY ever bind to a span
1355	# that already passes the full bar and therefore CANNOT introduce an
1356	# unsupported / fabricated claim.
1357	#
1358	# Default-OFF: when PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR is falsy the re-anchor is a no-op
1359	# and behaviour is BYTE-IDENTICAL to before (matches the gap-#18 /
1360	# PG_VERIFICATION_MODE precedent). It is ALSO gated on entailment ==
1361	# "enforce": under off/warn the reused verifier accepts on numeric +
1362	# content-overlap ALONE with NO enforced entailment bind, and because the
1363	# re-anchor ACTIVELY SEARCHES up to MAX_CANDIDATES windows for a coincidental
1364	# mechanical match, accepting under off/warn would launder a drop into a pass
1365	# (the §-1.1 lethal failure mode). So accept is permitted ONLY under enforce.
1366	
1367	PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR_MAX_CANDIDATES = int(
1368	    os.getenv("PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR_MAX_CANDIDATES", "40")
1369	)
1370	
1371	# Sliding-window size (chars) for enumerating candidate spans inside a row's
1372	# direct_quote. Mirrors the _find_local_support_window / _find_local_content_window
1373	# discipline (window=400) so a candidate is a bounded local slice, never the
1374	# whole row.
1375	PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR_WINDOW = int(
1376	    os.getenv("PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR_WINDOW", "400")
1377	)
1378

1380	def _provenance_reanchor_enabled() -> bool:
1381	    """True iff PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR is set truthy. Read at call time so
1382	    tests can toggle without re-import. Falsy (unset/0/false/no/off) => the
1383	    re-anchor is a no-op and strict_verify is byte-identical to pre-#1189."""
1384	    v = os.getenv("PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR", "").strip().lower()
1385	    return v in ("1", "true", "yes", "on", "enabled")
1386

1388	# I-perm-004 (#1198) slice 2 — when PG_SPAN_RESOLVER is truthy, the re-anchor recovery uses the
1389	# span_resolver BOILERPLATE-AWARE ARGMAX (best entailing prose span) instead of accepting the FIRST
1390	# passing candidate in enumeration order (which let drb_76 rebind to the row TITLE). Default OFF =>
1391	# the existing first-passing loop is byte-identical. The argmax still accepts ONLY a candidate that
1392	# passes the SAME full gate (verify_sentence_provenance, allow_local_window_fallback=False) — the
1393	# resolver's judge IS that gate — so no new fabrication path; it only CHOOSES a better span among
1394	# the ones that already pass.
1395	def _span_resolver_enabled() -> bool:
1396	    """True iff PG_SPAN_RESOLVER is set truthy (default OFF -> first-passing loop, byte-identical)."""
1397	    v = os.getenv("PG_SPAN_RESOLVER", "").strip().lower()
1398	    return v in ("1", "true", "yes", "on", "enabled")

1401	def _span_resolve_topk() -> int:

1410	# reset by the sweep. ONLY mutated inside the flag-on path, so OFF-mode never
1411	# touches them (byte-identity).
1412	_REANCHOR_TELEMETRY: dict[str, int] = {
1413	    "reanchor_attempts": 0,
1414	    "reanchor_recovered": 0,
1415	    "reanchor_uncited_bound": 0,

1419	    # (the reanchored_local_window soft-warning site). On gate-B allow_local_window_fallback is pinned OFF
1420	    # (:1580/:1607) so this leg is structurally unreachable => the counter is 0; the activation canary
1421	    # surfaces it as the provenance marker's ``local_window=<N>`` field and FAILS if a regression re-opens

1432	def reset_reanchor_telemetry() -> None:
1433	    """Zero the re-anchor counters (call between runs / tests)."""
1434	    for k in _REANCHOR_TELEMETRY:
1435	        _REANCHOR_TELEMETRY[k] = 0

1437	
1438	# I-complete-003 iter-2 (#1189) P2-1: DECIMAL-AWARE sentence terminator. A period
1439	# (``.``) that sits BETWEEN two digits (``1.5``, ``23.5``) is a decimal point, NOT
1440	# a sentence boundary — segmenting on it would split a decimal number across two
1441	# candidate spans, so the decimal-bearing support falls through to the sliding
1442	# window (and, for short rows, to a whole-row rebind that weakens citation
1443	# precision). ``!``/``?`` and a period NOT flanked by digits remain terminators.
1444	_REANCHOR_SENTENCE_TERMINATOR_RE = re.compile(r"(?<!\d)[.](?!\d)|[!?]")
1445	
1446	
1447	def _is_word_char(ch: str) -> bool:
1448	    """A word-constituent char for span-start snapping: a letter/digit or underscore."""
1449	    return ch.isalnum() or ch == "_"
1450	
1451	
1452	def _snap_start_to_word_boundary(text: str, start: int) -> int:
1453	    """Snap a span START offset back to the head of the word it lands inside, so a
1454	    recovered re-anchor fragment BEGINS AT A WHOLE WORD (never "...ptember 2025").
1455	
1456	    I-wire-013 (#1327): the sliding-window candidate branch below steps by a fixed
1457	    offset and so can land MID-TOKEN. Moving the start to the nearest PRECEDING word
1458	    boundary makes the emitted span open at a word. It only moves when ``start`` is
1459	    strictly INSIDE a word (the char before AND at ``start`` are both word chars); a
1460	    start already at whitespace / punctuation / index 0 is returned unchanged. PURE.
1461	    It only WIDENS the span leftward (start can decrease, never increase), so the
1462	    slice stays a valid ``0 <= start < end`` bound and the FROZEN strict_verify
1463	    span-bounds / entailment acceptance gate is unaffected (a wider candidate is
1464	    still judged by the SAME reused verifier; the render-side mid-word-start
1465	    backstop in weighted_enrichment stays in place as a second line of defence)."""
1466	    n = len(text)
1467	    if start <= 0 or start >= n:
1468	        return max(0, min(start, n))
1469	    if _is_word_char(text[start - 1]) and _is_word_char(text[start]):
1470	        i = start
1471	        while i > 0 and _is_word_char(text[i - 1]):
1472	            i -= 1
1473	        return i
1474	    return start
1475	
1476	
1477	def _reanchor_candidate_spans(direct_quote: str) -> list[tuple[int, int]]:
1478	    """Enumerate a BOUNDED set of candidate (start, end) spans inside a row's
1479	    ``direct_quote``.
1480	
1481	    Candidates are produced by (a) DECIMAL-AWARE sentence-segmenting the row text
1482	    and (b) a sliding window of PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR_WINDOW chars stepped by
1483	    half the window. Both are clamped to PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR_MAX_CANDIDATES
1484	    total so there is NO compute blow-up on a large row. Each span is a valid
1485	    ``0 <= start < end <= len(direct_quote)`` slice, so the reused verifier's
1486	    span-bounds checks pass naturally.
1487	
1488	    I-complete-003 iter-2 (#1189) P2-1:
1489	      * segmentation does NOT split a decimal number at its period (a period
1490	        between two digits is a decimal point, not a terminator), so a sentence
1491	        like ``...reduction of 1.5 percent.`` stays in ONE candidate segment;
1492	      * the DEGENERATE whole-row sliding-window candidate is SUPPRESSED for rows
1493	        shorter than the window — re-binding the token to the WHOLE row weakens
1494	        the claimed citation precision (it is not a tight supporting span). A
1495	        sentence SEGMENT that legitimately spans the entire row (a single-sentence
1496	        verbatim lift) is still emitted by branch (a), so genuine recovery on a
1497	        one-sentence row is preserved.
1498	    """
1499	    if not direct_quote:
1500	        return []
1501	    n = len(direct_quote)
1502	    cap = max(1, PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR_MAX_CANDIDATES)
1503	    seen: set[tuple[int, int]] = set()
1504	    candidates: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
1505	
1506	    def _add(start: int, end: int) -> None:
1507	        # I-wire-013 (#1327): snap a mid-token START to the head of its word so the
1508	        # recovered fragment opens at a word (no-op for branch-(a) segment starts,
1509	        # which already sit at index 0 / a post-terminator whitespace boundary).
1510	        start = _snap_start_to_word_boundary(direct_quote, max(0, start))
1511	        end = min(n, end)
1512	        if start >= end:
1513	            return
1514	        key = (start, end)
1515	        if key in seen:
1516	            return
1517	        seen.add(key)
1518	        candidates.append(key)
1519	
1520	    # (a) DECIMAL-AWARE sentence-segment candidates: split on sentence
1521	    # terminators that are NOT decimal points, keeping the trailing terminator
1522	    # with its segment. Each non-blank segment becomes a candidate span.
1523	    seg_start = 0
1524	    for m in _REANCHOR_SENTENCE_TERMINATOR_RE.finditer(direct_quote):
1525	        if len(candidates) >= cap:
1526	            return candidates[:cap]
1527	        seg_end = m.end()  # include the terminator char in the segment
1528	        if direct_quote[seg_start:seg_end].strip():
1529	            _add(seg_start, seg_end)
1530	        seg_start = seg_end
1531	    # Trailing segment (no terminating punctuation at end of row).
1532	    if seg_start < n and direct_quote[seg_start:n].strip() and len(candidates) < cap:
1533	        _add(seg_start, n)
1534	
1535	    # (b) Sliding-window candidates (half-window step) over the full row, to
1536	    # catch support that straddles sentence boundaries. P2-1: SKIP the degenerate
1537	    # whole-row window — when the window is >= the row length the only candidate
1538	    # the sliding loop would add is (0, n), a whole-row rebind that weakens
1539	    # citation precision. Branch (a) already supplies any legitimate full-row
1540	    # SENTENCE segment, so genuine recovery is unaffected.
1541	    window = max(1, PG_PROVENANCE_REANCHOR_WINDOW)
1542	    if window < n:
1543	        step = max(1, window // 2)
1544	        pos = 0
1545	        while pos < n and len(candidates) < cap:
1546	            _add(pos, pos + window)
1547	            pos += step
1548	
1549	    return candidates[:cap]
1550

1552	def _rebind_single_token(sentence: str, evidence_id: str, start: int, end: int) -> str:
1553	    """Rewrite the sentence's [#ev:...] provenance token(s) to a new
1554	    (evidence_id, start, end) span. SCOPE (v1, per ledger): single-token
1555	    re-anchor — every [#ev:...] occurrence is rewritten to the same rescued
1556	    span. Multi-token sentences with a UNION numeric failure
1557	    (which-token-to-move combinatorics) are explicitly OUT-OF-SCOPE for v1 and
1558	    are filtered out by the caller before this is reached."""
1559	    return _PROVENANCE_TOKEN_RE.sub(
1560	        f"[#ev:{evidence_id}:{start}-{end}]", sentence,
1561	    )

1564	def _try_reanchor(
1565	    sentence: str,
1566	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
1567	    *,
1568	    require_number_match: bool,
1569	    quantified_models: dict[tuple[str, str], Any] | None,
1570	) -> Optional[SentenceVerification]:
1571	    """I-complete-003 (#1189) — attempt to RE-ANCHOR a sentence that just
1572	    FAILED ``verify_sentence_provenance`` on its currently-cited span.
1573	
1574	    Returns a RECOVERED ``SentenceVerification`` (token re-bound, is_verified=
1575	    True) when a candidate span in the relevant row passes the FULL reused
1576	    gate, else ``None`` (caller keeps the existing drop). NO recursion: this
1577	    is invoked from the ``strict_verify`` caller loop, and the reused
1578	    ``verify_sentence_provenance`` is the SAME single acceptance entry point —
1579	    not re-implemented and not called from inside itself.
1580	
1581	    HARD CONSTRAINTS:
1582	      * accept ONLY under entailment ``enforce`` (the search-for-a-match shape
1583	        would otherwise launder a drop into a pass under off/warn);
1584	      * candidates are bounded (<= MAX_CANDIDATES) per row;
1585	      * v1 scope = single-token (cited) OR uncited verbatim-lift; multi-token
1586	        union-numeric is out-of-scope (returns None).
1587	    """
1588	    # Enforce-only accept gate (faithfulness-critical, mirrors gap-#18 L1407).
1589	    from src.polaris_graph.clinical_generator.strict_verify import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1590	        _entailment_mode as _emode_reanchor,
1591	    )
1592	    if _emode_reanchor() != "enforce":
1593	        return None
1594	
1595	    tokens = parse_provenance_tokens(sentence)
1596	
1597	    # ---- Path 1: CITED sentence — re-anchor within its cited row(s) ----
1598	    if tokens:
1599	        # v1 scope: a SINGLE [#ev] token. I-complete-003 iter-2 (#1189) P2-2:
1600	        # the prior `len(distinct_ids) != 1` filter only caught MULTI-ID
1601	        # sentences, but `_rebind_single_token` rewrites EVERY [#ev] occurrence
1602	        # to one span — so a sentence with TWO same-id tokens citing DIFFERENT
1603	        # spans (e.g. [#ev:a:0-10] ... [#ev:a:50-60]) would have BOTH collapsed
1604	        # onto a single rescued span, silently discarding the second citation.
1605	        # An explicit single-token guard keeps the v1 scope honest: multi-token

1610	        distinct_ids = {t.evidence_id for t in tokens}
1611	        if len(distinct_ids) != 1:

1618	        if not direct_quote:

1620	        _REANCHOR_TELEMETRY["reanchor_attempts"] += 1

1622	        # I-perm-004 (#1198) slice 2: a candidate "passes" iff re-binding the token to its span and
1623	        # running the SAME full gate (content + numeric + entailment, allow_local_window_fallback=
1624	        # False) verifies. This closure is the binding judge handed to the resolver — so the resolver
1625	        # can only ever choose among spans that already pass this exact gate.
1626	        def _candidate_passes(_sentence: str, span: tuple[int, int], _span_text: str) -> bool:
1627	            cand = _rebind_single_token(sentence, evidence_id, span[0], span[1])
1628	            return verify_sentence_provenance(
1629	                cand, evidence_pool,
1630	                require_number_match=require_number_match,
1631	                quantified_models=quantified_models,
1632	                allow_local_window_fallback=False,
1633	            ).is_verified
1634	
1635	        if _span_resolver_enabled():
1636	            # BOILERPLATE-AWARE ARGMAX: choose the best ENTAILING prose span instead of the first
1637	            # passing candidate in enumeration order (drb_76 rebound to the TITLE). Bounded judge
1638	            # calls (top_k). A title-only-supported claim is still recovered but LABELED with its
1639	            # provenance_quality so the report ships it caveated, never silently high-confidence.
1640	            from src.polaris_graph.generator.span_resolver import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1641	                resolve_best_entailing_span,
1642	            )
1643	            best = resolve_best_entailing_span(
1644	                direct_quote,
1645	                sentence,
1646	                _reanchor_candidate_spans(direct_quote),
1647	                judge_fn=_candidate_passes,
1648	                top_k=_span_resolve_topk(),
1649	            )
1650	            if best is None:
1651	                return None
1652	            rebound = _rebind_single_token(
1653	                sentence, evidence_id, best.best_span[0], best.best_span[1],
1654	            )
1655	            v = verify_sentence_provenance(
1656	                rebound, evidence_pool,
1657	                require_number_match=require_number_match,
1658	                quantified_models=quantified_models,
1659	                allow_local_window_fallback=False,
1660	            )
1661	            if not v.is_verified:  # defensive: the judge already passed this span; never launder.
1662	                return None
1663	            _REANCHOR_TELEMETRY["reanchor_recovered"] += 1
1664	            _REANCHOR_TELEMETRY["reanchor_argmax_recovered"] += 1
1665	            v.soft_warnings = list(v.soft_warnings) + [
1666	                f"reanchored_argmax:{evidence_id}:{best.best_span[0]}-{best.best_span[1]}:"
1667	                f"q={best.provenance_quality}:c={best.confidence:.2f}",
1668	            ]
1669	            return v
1670	
1671	        for (cand_start, cand_end) in _reanchor_candidate_spans(direct_quote):
1672	            rebound = _rebind_single_token(
1673	                sentence, evidence_id, cand_start, cand_end,
1674	            )
1675	            # I-complete-003 iter-2 (#1189) P1: the FINAL BOUND SPAN ITSELF must
1676	            # directly support — allow_local_window_fallback=False forbids a
1677	            # different in-row window from rescuing this candidate. So a candidate
1678	            # passes ONLY if its OWN span clears content+numeric AND directly
1679	            # entails, closing the Codex iter-1 leak.
1680	            v = verify_sentence_provenance(
1681	                rebound, evidence_pool,
1682	                require_number_match=require_number_match,
1683	                quantified_models=quantified_models,
1684	                allow_local_window_fallback=False,
1685	            )
1686	            if v.is_verified:
1687	                _REANCHOR_TELEMETRY["reanchor_recovered"] += 1
1688	                v.soft_warnings = list(v.soft_warnings) + [
1689	                    f"reanchored:{evidence_id}:{cand_start}-{cand_end}",
1690	                ]
1691	                return v
1692	        return None
1693	
1694	    # ---- Path 2: UNCITED sentence — find the pool row that verbatim-grounds it ----
1695	    # No [#ev] token: search the pool for the row whose direct_quote/text
1696	    # contains the verbatim (case-insensitive) sentence prose, then re-anchor
1697	    # within that row. Same full-gate bar applies.
1698	    bare = _verifier_cleaned_text(sentence).strip()
1699	    if not bare:
1700	        return None
1701	    bare_lower = bare.lower()
1702	    for evidence_id, ev in evidence_pool.items():
1703	        if not isinstance(ev, dict):
1704	            continue
1705	        direct_quote = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
1706	        if not direct_quote:
1707	            continue
1708	        if bare_lower not in direct_quote.lower():
1709	            continue
1710	        _REANCHOR_TELEMETRY["reanchor_attempts"] += 1
1711	        for (cand_start, cand_end) in _reanchor_candidate_spans(direct_quote):
1712	            # Append a fresh token (the sentence had none) and verify.
1713	            candidate_sentence = (
1714	                f"{sentence.rstrip()} [#ev:{evidence_id}:{cand_start}-{cand_end}]"
1715	            )
1716	            # I-complete-003 iter-2 (#1189) P1: same bound-span-itself-supports
1717	            # invariant on the uncited path — no in-row window rescue.
1718	            v = verify_sentence_provenance(
1719	                candidate_sentence, evidence_pool,
1720	                require_number_match=require_number_match,
1721	                quantified_models=quantified_models,
1722	                allow_local_window_fallback=False,
1723	            )
1724	            if v.is_verified:
1725	                _REANCHOR_TELEMETRY["reanchor_recovered"] += 1
1726	                _REANCHOR_TELEMETRY["reanchor_uncited_bound"] += 1
1727	                v.soft_warnings = list(v.soft_warnings) + [
1728	                    f"reanchored_uncited:{evidence_id}:{cand_start}-{cand_end}",
1729	                ]
1730	                return v
1731	        # Only the first verbatim-containing row is attempted (bounded).
1732	        return None
1733	
1734	    return None

1737	def _verification_mode() -> str:
1738	    """Phase 0b (I-meta-005, gap-#18): verification-mode router for the three
1739	    grounded-prose deltas. Read at call time so tests can override.
1740	
1741	      off (default) — byte-identical to pre-0b behavior; no delta fires.
1742	      shadow        — deltas DETECT + log telemetry but do NOT change
1743	                      is_verified, and make NO extra judge calls (spend-
1744	                      neutral free Gate-A measurement).
1745	      enforce       — deltas change is_verified (rescue grounded prose via a
1746	                      BOUNDED local content window; drop the judge-error
1747	                      fail-open sentinel).
1748	    """
1749	    v = os.getenv("PG_VERIFICATION_MODE", "off").strip().lower()
1750	    return v if v in ("off", "shadow", "enforce") else "off"

1753	# M-25a: trial-name match for strict_verify.
1754	#
1755	# DR audit pass 4 FABRICATED #20: generator wrote "SURMOUNT-1 ... 20.9%
1756	# at 72 weeks versus 3.1% placebo" and bound it to evidence ev_015 whose
1757	# title is "Tirzepatide after intensive lifestyle intervention: the
1758	# SURMOUNT-3 phase 3 trial". The old verifier passed the binding because
1759	# the content words {tirzepatide, surmount} overlap and some placebo-arm
1760	# percentage happened to appear in the SURMOUNT-3 span body. But the
1761	# trial identity was wrong — SURMOUNT-1 is not SURMOUNT-3.
1762	#
1763	# We extract named trials (SURPASS-N, SURMOUNT-N, SURMOUNT-CN, STEP-N,
1764	# SELECT, LEADER, SUSTAIN, PIONEER, REWIND, AWARD, GRADE) as ATOMIC
1765	# tokens. If a sentence names trial T, at least one cited evidence row
1766	# must also mention T in its statement/title/direct_quote. Sentences
1767	# without any named trial are not gated by this check.
1768	#
1769	# Bare-word acronyms (SELECT, LEADER, SUSTAIN, PIONEER, REWIND, AWARD,
1770	# GRADE) are matched when they appear in ALLCAPS to avoid over-matching
1771	# common words.
1772	
1773	_TRIAL_NUMBERED_RE = re.compile(
1774	    # SURPASS-1 through SURPASS-99, SURMOUNT-1 through SURMOUNT-99, STEP-N
1775	    r"\b(?:SURPASS|SURMOUNT|STEP)-(?:[0-9]{1,2}|CN|OSA|AP|J|MMO)\b",
1776	    re.IGNORECASE,
1777	)
1778	
1779	_TRIAL_ALLCAPS_RE = re.compile(
1780	    # Named trial programs that must be distinguished from common words
1781	    # by ALLCAPS presentation in the sentence/evidence.
1782	    r"\b(?:SELECT|LEADER|SUSTAIN|PIONEER|REWIND|AWARD|GRADE)\b",
1783	)
1784	
1785	
1786	def extract_trial_names(text: str) -> set[str]:
1787	    """Return the set of trial-program names (normalized to uppercase
1788	    with hyphen) found in the text. Used by the M-25a trial-name gate.
1789	
1790	    Numbered trials (SURPASS-2, SURMOUNT-3, STEP-1, SURMOUNT-CN) are
1791	    case-insensitive. Bare-word trial names (SELECT, LEADER, etc.)
1792	    are ALLCAPS-only to avoid matching common words.
1793	    """
1794	    if not text:
1795	        return set()
1796	    found: set[str] = set()
1797	    for m in _TRIAL_NUMBERED_RE.finditer(text):
1798	        # Normalize: uppercase the trial root and preserve dash-suffix case.
1799	        raw = m.group(0)
1800	        head, _, tail = raw.partition("-")
1801	        found.add(f"{head.upper()}-{tail.upper()}")
1802	    for m in _TRIAL_ALLCAPS_RE.finditer(text):
1803	        found.add(m.group(0).upper())
1804	    return found
1805	
1806	
1807	def _trial_names_in_evidence(ev: dict[str, Any]) -> set[str]:
1808	    """Pull trial names from an evidence row's AUTHORITATIVE identity
1809	    fields — statement (title/summary) and title — but NOT direct_quote.
1810	
1811	    DR pass 7 (2026-04-20) demonstrated that scanning direct_quote is
1812	    too permissive: the SURMOUNT-3 Nature paper's direct_quote cited
1813	    SURMOUNT-1 as a prior reference, which let a fabricated
1814	    'In SURMOUNT-1, ...' sentence pass the trial-name gate when bound
1815	    to ev_015 (SURMOUNT-3). statement + title encode the AUTHORITATIVE
1816	    trial identity of the paper; direct_quote encodes what the paper
1817	    DISCUSSES, which legitimately spans other trials for context.
1818	    """
1819	    if not ev:
1820	        return set()
1821	    acc: set[str] = set()
1822	    # M-25a hardening (pass 7): title/statement only. direct_quote
1823	    # excluded — it's too permissive for trial-name identity matching.
1824	    for key in ("statement", "title"):
1825	        val = ev.get(key) or ""
1826	        if val:
1827	            acc |= extract_trial_names(val)
1828	    return acc
1829	
1830	
1831	def _trial_name_span_fallback_enabled() -> bool:
1832	    """I-meta-002-q1d (#949). Default ON. When OFF, trial-name matching is the exact pass-7
1833	    title/statement-only behavior (byte-identical)."""
1834	    return os.getenv("PG_VERIFY_TRIAL_NAME_SPAN_FALLBACK", "1").strip().lower() not in (
1835	        "0", "false", "no", "off", "",
1836	    )
1837	
1838	
1839	def _trial_names_for_cited_row(ev: dict[str, Any], cited_spans: list[tuple[int, int]]) -> set[str]:
1840	    """Trial names this cited row authoritatively contributes (I-meta-002-q1d #949).
1841	
1842	    TITLE AUTHORITY (the binding clinical-safety rule): if statement/title names ANY trial, that set is
1843	    returned and the span is NOT consulted — a row whose title declares trial T can never match a sentence
1844	    naming a different trial, regardless of what its direct_quote/body contains (this preserves the pass-7
1845	    FABRICATED-#20 locked-FAIL even when the citation span covers the whole body).
1846	
1847	    SPAN FALLBACK (only when the title/statement names NO trial — the SURPASS-2-omitted-from-title case):
1848	    return trial names found in the CITED SPANS ONLY (`direct_quote[start:end]` for this row's tokens — the
1849	    same slice the numeric/content checks use), NOT the whole direct_quote. The cited RESULTS span names the
1850	    trial whose result it states; a prior-reference mention OUTSIDE the cited span never matches, and a body
1851	    that contextualises against sibling trials elsewhere does not pollute the match. Gated by
1852	    `_trial_name_span_fallback_enabled()` (default ON; OFF → exact title-only behavior)."""
1853	    title_trials = _trial_names_in_evidence(ev)
1854	    if title_trials or not _trial_name_span_fallback_enabled():
1855	        return title_trials
1856	    direct_quote = (ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or "") if ev else ""
1857	    if not direct_quote:
1858	        return set()
1859	    span_trials: set[str] = set()
1860	    for start, end in cited_spans:
1861	        span_trials |= extract_trial_names(direct_quote[start:end])
1862	    return span_trials

1865	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1866	# Regime C — verification of COMPUTED numbers (Phase 7, #991)
1867	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1868	# Captures the numeric run IMMEDIATELY before a calc token (optional $ prefix,
1869	# optional % suffix, thousands-grouped). Used for token-adjacency binding so the
1870	# token verifies against the number it directly follows, not a modeled-assumption
1871	# number elsewhere in the sentence.
1872	_CALC_ADJACENT_NUMBER_RE = re.compile(r"(\$?\s*-?\d[\d,]*(?:\.\d+)?\s*%?)\s*$")
1873	
1874	
1875	def _calc_parse_number(text: str) -> float | None:
1876	    m = re.search(r"-?\d[\d,]*(?:\.\d+)?", text or "")
1877	    if not m:
1878	        return None
1879	    try:
1880	        return float(m.group(0).replace(",", ""))
1881	    except ValueError:
1882	        return None
1883	
1884	
1885	def verify_modeled_atom(
1886	    sentence: str,
1887	    calc_match: "re.Match[str]",
1888	    quantified_models: dict[tuple[str, str], Any],
1889	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
1890	) -> SentenceVerification:
1891	    """Verify a sentence carrying a ``[#calc:model_id:spec_hash:field]`` token.
1892	
1893	    Fail-closed checks (brief §1.5):
1894	      (a) (model_id, spec_hash) resolves in the RUN-SCOPED registry — a stale or
1895	          foreign model reference fails (P7-19).
1896	      (b) the field exists in that model's executed result.
1897	      (c) the rendered number IMMEDIATELY before the token equals the field's
1898	          canonical ``display_value`` — exact display-string match OR a tight
1899	          numeric backstop (D2). Adjacency means a modeled-assumption number
1900	          elsewhere in the sentence cannot satisfy the token (P7-20).
1901	      (d) every modeled input USED by the field is labeled "(modeled assumption)"
1902	          in the sentence (P7-7/P7-8).
1903	      (e) the field's sourced inputs all resolve in the evidence pool.
1904	
1905	    On PASS, returns SentenceVerification whose ``tokens`` are the source-input
1906	    ProvenanceTokens (so resolve_provenance_to_citations cites the inputs) — the
1907	    calc token is stripped downstream. On any failure → is_verified=False (the
1908	    whole sentence is dropped).
1909	    """
1910	    model_id = calc_match.group("model_id")
1911	    spec_hash = calc_match.group("spec_hash")
1912	    field_id = calc_match.group("field")
1913	    failures: list[str] = []
1914	
1915	    result = quantified_models.get((model_id, spec_hash))
1916	    if result is None:
1917	        return SentenceVerification(
1918	            sentence=sentence, tokens=[], is_verified=False,
1919	            failure_reasons=[f"calc_model_not_in_registry:{model_id}:{spec_hash}"],
1920	        )
1921	    fld = getattr(result, "fields", {}).get(field_id)
1922	    if not isinstance(fld, dict):
1923	        return SentenceVerification(
1924	            sentence=sentence, tokens=[], is_verified=False,
1925	            failure_reasons=[f"calc_field_unknown:{field_id}"],
1926	        )
1927	
1928	    display_value = str(fld.get("display_value", ""))
1929	
1930	    # (c) adjacency + equality (Codex diff-gate P1-1/P1-2). The rendered number
1931	    # IMMEDIATELY before the token must canonicalize to EXACTLY the field's
1932	    # display_value. Two fail-closed properties:
1933	    #   - FULL-number compare (not `before.endswith(display_value)`, which would
1934	    #     accept "123.40%" for a "23.40%" field — the wrong number ends with the
1935	    #     canonical string).
1936	    #   - RE-CANONICALIZE the parsed adjacent value through the SAME pinned
1937	    #     formatter and require an exact string match (no magnitude-scaled rel-tol
1938	    #     drift, which would accept "$1,000,000,000,999.00" for a
1939	    #     "$1,000,000,000,000.00" field). A benign reformat (missing $/commas) that
1940	    #     canonicalizes to the same string still passes; any real numeric
1941	    #     difference fails.
1942	    before = sentence[: calc_match.start()]
1943	    adj = _CALC_ADJACENT_NUMBER_RE.search(before)
1944	    if adj is None:
1945	        failures.append("calc_no_adjacent_number")
1946	    else:
1947	        adj_str = adj.group(1).strip()
1948	        ok = adj_str == display_value
1949	        if not ok:
1950	            adj_val = _calc_parse_number(adj_str)
1951	            if adj_val is not None:
1952	                from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import (
1953	                    _canonical_display,
1954	                )
1955	                recanon = _canonical_display(
1956	                    adj_val,
1957	                    str(fld.get("unit", "")),
1958	                    str(fld.get("display_kind", "number")),
1959	                )
1960	                ok = recanon == display_value
1961	        if not ok:
1962	            failures.append(f"calc_number_mismatch:{adj_str}!={display_value}")
1963	
1964	    # (d) modeled-assumption labeling
1965	    modeled_used = fld.get("modeled_used") or []
1966	    if modeled_used and _MODELED_ASSUMPTION_LABEL not in sentence:
1967	        failures.append(
1968	            "calc_modeled_assumption_unlabeled:" + ",".join(map(str, modeled_used))
1969	        )
1970	
1971	    # (e) sourced-input evidence resolution + ProvenanceToken construction
1972	    src_tokens: list[ProvenanceToken] = []
1973	    for t in (fld.get("sourced_tokens") or []):
1974	        ev_id = str(t.get("ev_id", ""))
1975	        if ev_id not in evidence_pool:
1976	            failures.append(f"calc_input_ev_not_in_pool:{ev_id}")
1977	            continue
1978	        src_tokens.append(ProvenanceToken(
1979	            evidence_id=ev_id,
1980	            start=int(t.get("start", 0)),
1981	            end=int(t.get("end", 0)),
1982	            raw=str(t.get("raw", "")),
1983	        ))
1984	
1985	    if failures:
1986	        return SentenceVerification(
1987	            sentence=sentence, tokens=[], is_verified=False,
1988	            failure_reasons=failures,
1989	        )
1990	    return SentenceVerification(
1991	        sentence=sentence, tokens=src_tokens, is_verified=True,
1992	        failure_reasons=[], soft_warnings=["regime_c_modeled_verified"],
1993	    )

1996	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1997	# I-deepfix-001 V6 (#1344): author-attribution metadata binding for the entailment leg.
1998	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1999	# ROOT (grounded in drb_72_ai_labor/verification_details.json): the entailment judge
2000	# NEUTRAL-dropped a genuinely-grounded finding —
2001	#   sentence: "Eloundou and colleagues propose a framework ... [#ev:eloundou_gpts_are_gpts:0-800]."
2002	#   reason:   "entailment_failed:...verdict=NEUTRAL:reason=The sentence adds the
2003	#              unsupported specificity of the authors' names (Eloundou and ...)"
2004	# The cited evidence row carries authors=['Eloundou T','Manning S','Mishkin P','Rock D']
2005	# as REAL bibliographic metadata, but the fetched `direct_quote` span omits the byline,
2006	# so the judge — which only sees the cited SPAN text — treats the author attribution as
2007	# unsupported specificity and the sentence drops.
2008	#
2009	# FIX: when the SENTENCE names an author who is genuinely in a cited source's `authors`
2010	# metadata, append that source's real author list to the SPAN TEXT PASSED TO THE
2011	# ENTAILMENT JUDGE ONLY, so a true attribution is groundable. §-1.3-safe / faithfulness-
2012	# TIGHTENING-not-relaxing: it binds ONLY REAL metadata from the evidence record (never a
2013	# claim, never a number, never a topic), and ONLY when the sentence actually names one of
2014	# the real authors — a fabricated attribution ("Smith found ...", no such author) never
2015	# matches, so it still NEUTRAL-drops. The mechanical numeric/content-overlap gates run
2016	# EARLIER against the span alone and are unaffected. LAW VI kill-switch:
2017	# PG_ENTAILMENT_AUTHOR_METADATA_BIND=0 reverts byte-identical (no annotation appended).
2018	_AUTHOR_METADATA_BIND_ENV = "PG_ENTAILMENT_AUTHOR_METADATA_BIND"
2019	# A surname must be at least this long to be matched as a whole word in the sentence
2020	# (guards against a 2-3 char initial/common-word false match, e.g. "Rock D" -> "Rock").
2021	_AUTHOR_SURNAME_MIN_LEN = 4
2022	_AUTHOR_NAME_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z'’\-]+")
2023	_AUTHOR_SPLIT_RE = re.compile(r"\s*(?:;|,| and | & )\s*")

2026	def _author_metadata_bind_enabled() -> bool:
2027	    """PG_ENTAILMENT_AUTHOR_METADATA_BIND gate (default ON)."""
2028	    return os.getenv(_AUTHOR_METADATA_BIND_ENV, "1").strip().lower() not in (
2029	        "", "0", "false", "off", "no",

2032	
2033	def _author_entries(authors: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
2034	    """(surname, full_entry) pairs from an evidence row's ``authors`` metadata.
2035	
2036	    Accepts a list of author strings or a single delimited string. The surname is the
2037	    longest alphabetic token >= _AUTHOR_SURNAME_MIN_LEN in each entry ("Eloundou T" ->
2038	    "Eloundou"; "J. Satyadhar" -> "Satyadhar"). Entries with no long-enough token are
2039	    skipped (cannot be matched safely)."""
2040	    if not authors:
2041	        return []
2042	    if isinstance(authors, str):
2043	        entries = [a for a in _AUTHOR_SPLIT_RE.split(authors) if a and a.strip()]
2044	    elif isinstance(authors, (list, tuple)):
2045	        entries = [str(a) for a in authors if str(a).strip()]
2046	    else:
2047	        return []
2048	    out: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
2049	    for entry in entries:
2050	        entry = entry.strip()
2051	        toks = [t for t in _AUTHOR_NAME_TOKEN_RE.findall(entry) if len(t) >= _AUTHOR_SURNAME_MIN_LEN]
2052	        if not toks:
2053	            continue
2054	        surname = max(toks, key=len)
2055	        out.append((surname, entry))
2056	    return out
2057	
2058	
2059	def _author_metadata_annotation(
2060	    sentence: str,
2061	    tokens: list["ProvenanceToken"],
2062	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
2063	) -> str:
2064	    """Bibliographic-metadata line binding the REAL authors of a cited source when the
2065	    SENTENCE names one of them, else "" (byte-identical / no augmentation).
2066	
2067	    Fires per cited source: if any of that source's author surnames appears as a whole
2068	    word in ``sentence``, the source's FULL author list is appended (so "Eloundou and
2069	    colleagues" is grounded by both Eloundou and the co-authors). Returns "" when the
2070	    leg is disabled, the sentence names no real author, or no cited source carries
2071	    author metadata."""
2072	    if not _author_metadata_bind_enabled() or not sentence or not tokens:
2073	        return ""
2074	    seen_ids: set[str] = set()
2075	    lists: list[str] = []
2076	    for tok in tokens:
2077	        ev = evidence_pool.get(tok.evidence_id)
2078	        if ev is None or tok.evidence_id in seen_ids:
2079	            continue
2080	        seen_ids.add(tok.evidence_id)
2081	        pairs = _author_entries(ev.get("authors") if isinstance(ev, dict) else None)
2082	        if not pairs:
2083	            continue
2084	        named = any(
2085	            re.search(rf"\b{re.escape(surname)}\b", sentence) for surname, _ in pairs
2086	        )
2087	        if named:
2088	            full_list = ", ".join(full for _, full in pairs)
2089	            if full_list:
2090	                lists.append(full_list)
2091	    if not lists:
2092	        return ""
2093	    # dedupe preserving order (two cited spans of the SAME paper -> one metadata line)
2094	    uniq = list(dict.fromkeys(lists))
2095	    return " Cited source authors: " + "; ".join(uniq) + "."
2096

2901	    # error_flag is only ever set inside the entailment block (warn/enforce), so re-reading the entailment

2903	    if judge_error_flag:

2946	        # BYTE-UNCHANGED — this is an observability counter, NOT a change to the verify/recover logic.
2947	        _REANCHOR_TELEMETRY["reanchor_local_window_recovered"] += 1
2948	        soft_warnings = list(soft_warnings) + [
2949	            f"reanchored_local_window:{_rev_id}:{_rev_start}-{_rev_end}",
2950	        ]

2952	    return SentenceVerification(
2953	        sentence=final_sentence,
2954	        tokens=final_tokens,
2955	        is_verified=is_verified,

2958	        judge_error=judge_error_flag,

2963	# Strict mode: drop un-verified sentences

2966	
2967	_SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE = re.compile(
2968	    # Two alternatives for sentence boundary:
2969	    #   (1) `.!?` directly followed by whitespace + capital/bracket
2970	    #   (2) `]` (end of citation marker) followed by whitespace + capital,
2971	    #       which handles `"Drug works.[1] Next sentence."`
2972	    r"(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z\[])|(?<=\])\s+(?=[A-Z])",
2973	)
2974	
2975	# I-deepfix-006 splitter widening (#1376) — PG_SENTENCE_SPLIT_SYMBOL_BOUNDARY. A Franken-sentence often
2976	# glues two source sentences where the second starts with a SYMBOL or a DIGIT rather than a capital
2977	# ("… adoption.* Bullet furniture", "… 2020. 15 firms reported", '… rate. "Next" said'). The legacy
2978	# regex only splits before ``[A-Z\[]``, so the glue survives as one blob and a trailing furniture symbol
2979	# rides a real finding. This widens the split with THREE guarded legs:
2980	#   • hard furniture symbols ``* © •`` — whitespace OPTIONAL (they glue right after the period);
2981	#   • a DIGIT — whitespace MANDATORY, so a decimal ("3.75", no space) is NEVER split;
2982	#   • an opening / straight QUOTE — whitespace MANDATORY, so a glued CLOSING quote ("done." ) is NEVER
2983	#     split off its own sentence.
2984	# Direction TIGHTENS (more, shorter units — an un-tokened split unit is dropped by the frozen
2985	# strict_verify, isolating the furniture). Default-ON; byte-identical OFF.
2986	_SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE_V2 = re.compile(
2987	    r"(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[A-Z\[])"        # legacy: capital / bracket after whitespace
2988	    r"|(?<=\])\s+(?=[A-Z])"            # legacy: capital after a citation marker
2989	    r"|(?<=[.!?])\s*(?=[*©•])"         # hard furniture symbols — whitespace optional (glued)
2990	    r"|(?<=[.!?])\s+(?=[\d\"'“‘])",    # digit / opening quote — whitespace mandatory (protect decimals)
2991	)
2992	
2993	
2994	def _sentence_split_symbol_boundary_enabled() -> bool:
2995	    """PG_SENTENCE_SPLIT_SYMBOL_BOUNDARY (default ON). OFF / empty => the legacy split regex is used =>
2996	    byte-identical sentence segmentation."""
2997	    return os.getenv("PG_SENTENCE_SPLIT_SYMBOL_BOUNDARY", "1").strip().lower() not in (
2998	        "0", "false", "off", "no",
2999	    )
3000	
3001	
3002	def split_into_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
3003	    """Lightweight sentence splitter. Good enough for our generator output.
3004	
3005	    Handles trailing citation markers like `.[1]`, `.[#ev:ev_a:0-5]`,
3006	    `.[1][2]` — the second alternative in the split regex triggers on
3007	    the closing `]` so the marker stays attached to the preceding
3008	    sentence rather than being eaten.
3009	
3010	    I-deepfix-006 (#1376): when PG_SENTENCE_SPLIT_SYMBOL_BOUNDARY is ON (default) alternative (1) also
3011	    splits after ``.!?`` + whitespace before a symbol (``* © •`` / quote) or a digit, catching a
3012	    Franken-sentence glue the capital-only rule missed. OFF => byte-identical legacy segmentation.
3013	    """
3014	    if not text:
3015	        return []
3016	    rx = _SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE_V2 if _sentence_split_symbol_boundary_enabled() else _SENTENCE_SPLIT_RE
3017	    parts = rx.split(text.strip())
3018	    return [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]

3021	@dataclass
3022	class StrictVerificationReport:
3023	    kept_sentences: list[SentenceVerification]
3024	    dropped_sentences: list[SentenceVerification]
3025	    total_in: int
3026	    total_kept: int
3027	    total_dropped: int

3030	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3031	# B5/B7 (DUAL_AGREED_PLAN §B5/B7, operator-locked 2026-06-14) — strict_verify drop
3032	# DISPOSITION classifier + report-body disclosure.
3033	#
3034	# The plan's "FIX THE LIVE SILENT-DROP HOLE": strict_verify silently deletes failing
3035	# sentences. Distinguish two dispositions so the drop is NEVER silent — without ever
3036	# rendering a generator-hallucinated sentence as asserted prose:
3037	#
3038	#   * UN-PROVENANCED HARD DROP — the sentence carried no usable [#ev] provenance (or was
3039	#     content-empty). It is not a grounded claim; it is correctly DROPPED with no body
3040	#     content (only counted). These are hygiene drops.
3041	#   * SUPPORT-FAILED DISCLOSED DROP — the sentence DID carry provenance but failed span /
3042	#     numeric / overlap / entailment verification against its own source. This is the
3043	#     "unsupported/contradicted by source" class. The faithfulness rule is ABSOLUTE: the
3044	#     original (hallucinated/unsupported) sentence text MUST NOT render as a finding. The
3045	#     drop is surfaced as a DISCLOSED COUNT + reason, never as the raw failed sentence.
3046	#
3047	# This is faithfulness-NEUTRAL: no failed sentence is resurrected, no asserted prose is
3048	# added. It only converts a silent deletion into a counted, reasoned disclosure.
3049	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3050	
3051	# Reasons where the sentence had NO grounded claim to begin with -> hard hygiene drop.
3052	_UNPROVENANCED_DROP_REASONS = frozenset({
3053	    "no_provenance_token",
3054	    "empty_or_contentless_sentence",
3055	})
3056	
3057	DROP_DISPOSITION_UNPROVENANCED = "unprovenanced_hard_drop"
3058	DROP_DISPOSITION_SUPPORT_FAILED = "support_failed_disclosed"
3059	
3060	
3061	def classify_drop_disposition(sv: SentenceVerification) -> str:
3062	    """Classify a strict_verify-dropped ``SentenceVerification`` (B5/B7).
3063	
3064	    Returns ``DROP_DISPOSITION_UNPROVENANCED`` when the sentence had no usable provenance
3065	    (``no_provenance_token`` / ``empty_or_contentless_sentence``) — a hygiene drop with no
3066	    grounded claim. Returns ``DROP_DISPOSITION_SUPPORT_FAILED`` for EVERY other failure
3067	    reason (numeric_mismatch / overlap_too_low / entailment_failed / invalid_token /
3068	    span_out_of_range / calc_* / judge-error fail-closed): the sentence cited provenance but
3069	    failed verification against its own source — the "unsupported/contradicted by source"
3070	    class that must be DISCLOSED (count) but NEVER rendered as asserted prose.
3071	
3072	    Fail-safe: a sentence with NO failure_reasons but is_verified=False is treated as
3073	    support-failed (the stricter, disclosed bucket) — it is never silently nothing.
3074	    """
3075	    reasons = list(getattr(sv, "failure_reasons", None) or [])
3076	    # A dropped sentence is UN-provenanced only if EVERY reason is an unprovenanced reason
3077	    # (and there is at least one). Any support-grounding failure -> the disclosed bucket.
3078	    if reasons and all(r.split(":", 1)[0] in _UNPROVENANCED_DROP_REASONS for r in reasons):
3079	        return DROP_DISPOSITION_UNPROVENANCED
3080	    return DROP_DISPOSITION_SUPPORT_FAILED
3081	
3082	
3083	def build_drop_disclosure(dropped_sentences: list[SentenceVerification]) -> dict:
3084	    """Summarize strict_verify drops into a DISCLOSURE record (B5/B7) — counts + reasons only.
3085	
3086	    NEVER includes the raw dropped sentence text (a support-failed sentence is
3087	    generator-hallucinated / unsupported and must not ship as prose). Returns a dict:
3088	    ``{support_failed_count, unprovenanced_count, support_failed_reason_counts,
3089	    unprovenanced_reason_counts}``. Reason counts are SPLIT BY DISPOSITION (Codex diff-gate P2) so
3090	    the support-failed disclosure's reasons match its claim count exactly and are never conflated
3091	    with hygiene (un-provenanced) reasons. Reason keys collapse parameterized detail
3092	    (``reason:detail`` -> ``reason``). Both reason maps are empty when there are no drops of that
3093	    disposition.
3094	    """
3095	    support_failed = 0
3096	    unprovenanced = 0
3097	    support_failed_reason_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
3098	    unprovenanced_reason_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
3099	    for sv in (dropped_sentences or []):
3100	        disposition = classify_drop_disposition(sv)
3101	        if disposition == DROP_DISPOSITION_SUPPORT_FAILED:
3102	            support_failed += 1
3103	            target = support_failed_reason_counts
3104	        else:
3105	            unprovenanced += 1
3106	            target = unprovenanced_reason_counts
3107	        for r in (getattr(sv, "failure_reasons", None) or []):
3108	            key = r.split(":", 1)[0]
3109	            target[key] = target.get(key, 0) + 1
3110	    return {
3111	        "support_failed_count": support_failed,
3112	        "unprovenanced_count": unprovenanced,
3113	        "support_failed_reason_counts": support_failed_reason_counts,
3114	        "unprovenanced_reason_counts": unprovenanced_reason_counts,
3115	    }

3117	
3118	def render_full_drop_disclosure_md(
3119	    drop_summary: dict,
3120	    *,
3121	    dedup_redundant_count: int = 0,
3122	    m41c_underframed_count: int = 0,
3123	) -> str:
3124	    """B08 (#1352) finding #2: render the ## Evidence-support disclosure block from the FULL
3125	    drop accounting, not the support-failed subset only.
3126	
3127	    The prior render surfaced ONLY ``support_failed_count`` + its reasons, so a reader of
3128	    report.md saw "30 removed" when the run actually dropped support-failed +
3129	    un-provenanced (``no_provenance_token`` / ``empty_or_contentless_sentence``) +
3130	    dedup-redundant + M-41c under-framed sentences. This understated how much content was
3131	    removed and is the disclosure-to-render gap B08 fixes.
3132	
3133	    Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: every category below is a COUNT (+ reason tally) only — NO raw
3134	    dropped sentence text is ever rendered as prose (a support-failed sentence is
3135	    generator-hallucinated / unsupported and must not ship). It converts a partial
3136	    disclosure into the complete one; it never resurrects a dropped claim and never touches
3137	    a strict_verify / NLI / span / 4-role verdict.
3138	
3139	    ``drop_summary`` is the dict returned by :func:`build_drop_disclosure`. ``dedup_redundant``
3140	    sentences are LLM-consolidated near-duplicates (a CONSOLIDATION, not a faithfulness drop —
3141	    §-1.3 keep-all corroboration) and ``m41c_underframed`` sentences PASSED strict_verify but
3142	    were removed by the claim-frame policy filter; both are disclosed as distinct, named
3143	    categories so the reader can tell a removal apart from a verification failure.
3144	
3145	    Returns "" when there is NOTHING to disclose across ALL categories (so a clean run's
3146	    report.md is byte-identical to having no block).
3147	    """
3148	    support_failed = int(drop_summary.get("support_failed_count", 0) or 0)
3149	    unprovenanced = int(drop_summary.get("unprovenanced_count", 0) or 0)
3150	    dedup_redundant = int(dedup_redundant_count or 0)
3151	    m41c = int(m41c_underframed_count or 0)
3152	    total_removed = support_failed + unprovenanced + dedup_redundant + m41c
3153	    if total_removed <= 0:
3154	        return ""
3155	
3156	    lines: list[str] = [
3157	        "",
3158	        "",
3159	        "## Evidence-support disclosure",
3160	        "",
3161	        (
3162	            f"{total_removed} generated sentence(s) were REMOVED before the findings above "
3163	            "and are disclosed here rather than silently dropped. The total breaks down by "
3164	            "category:"
3165	        ),
3166	        "",
3167	    ]
3168	    if support_failed > 0:
3169	        reason_md = ", ".join(
3170	            f"{k}: {v}"
3171	            for k, v in sorted(
3172	                (drop_summary.get("support_failed_reason_counts", {}) or {}).items()
3173	            )
3174	        )
3175	        lines.append(
3176	            f"- Support-failed ({support_failed}): a claim was generated but did not pass "
3177	            "span/numeric/entailment verification against its OWN cited source, so it was "
3178	            "removed (not asserted as fact)."
3179	            + (f" Drop reasons: {reason_md}." if reason_md else "")
3180	            + " (Reason counts are tallied per failed verification check, so a claim that "
3181	            "failed more than one check is counted under each; the reason counts therefore "
3182	            "need not sum to the count above.)"
3183	        )
3184	    if unprovenanced > 0:
3185	        reason_md = ", ".join(
3186	            f"{k}: {v}"
3187	            for k, v in sorted(
3188	                (drop_summary.get("unprovenanced_reason_counts", {}) or {}).items()
3189	            )
3190	        )
3191	        lines.append(
3192	            f"- Un-provenanced ({unprovenanced}): a sentence carried no usable "
3193	            "provenance token (no grounded claim to verify), so it was dropped as a hygiene "
3194	            "removal."
3195	            + (f" Drop reasons: {reason_md}." if reason_md else "")
3196	        )
3197	    if dedup_redundant > 0:
3198	        lines.append(
3199	            f"- Dedup-redundant ({dedup_redundant}): a near-duplicate sentence carrying the "
3200	            "SAME claim as a kept sentence was consolidated away (corroboration is kept on the "
3201	            "surviving sentence; this is a de-duplication, not a verification failure)."
3202	        )
3203	    if m41c > 0:
3204	        lines.append(
3205	            f"- Claim-frame policy ({m41c}): a sentence PASSED span verification but was "
3206	            "removed by the under-framed-trial-name claim-frame filter."
3207	        )
3208	    lines.append("")
3209	    return "\n".join(lines)

3212	def build_d8_unadjudicated_banner(release_disclosure: "dict | None") -> str:
3213	    """B08 (#1352) finding #1: a run-specific top-of-report banner surfaced into report.md
3214	    when the strongest verifier (four-role D8) did NOT adjudicate this run.
3215	
3216	    The A18 always-release path serializes the honest ``adjudicated`` flag into
3217	    ``manifest['release_disclosure']`` — but a reader of report.md ALONE could not tell the
3218	    keystone verifier was skipped, because that disclosure lived only in manifest.json. This
3219	    helper renders the banner from that SAME serialized flag so report.md is honest on its own.
3220	
3221	    Returns the banner markdown ONLY when ``release_disclosure`` is a dict that explicitly
3222	    records ``adjudicated == False``. Returns "" when the disclosure is missing, malformed, or
3223	    records ``adjudicated`` truthy (a genuinely D8-judged release stays banner-free). The
3224	    helper asserts NO finding and touches NO faithfulness verdict — it is pure disclosure
3225	    plumbing.
3226	    """
3227	    if not isinstance(release_disclosure, dict):
3228	        return ""
3229	    # Strict identity (I-deepfix-001 Codex P2): emit the banner ONLY when
3230	    # adjudicated is EXPLICITLY False. A missing key or a malformed falsey value
3231	    # (None / 0 / "") must NOT trigger it — the contract is "explicit
3232	    # adjudicated == False", not "falsey".
3233	    if release_disclosure.get("adjudicated") is not False:
3234	        return ""
3235	    return (
3236	        "> **STRONGEST VERIFIER (four-role D8) DID NOT RUN for this run — findings are "
3237	        "UNVERIFIED-by-D8.**\n"
3238	        ">\n"
3239	        "> The four-role D8 adjudication (the strongest faithfulness verifier) did not bind "
3240	        "for this run, so the findings below carry only the strict_verify / span-grounding / "
3241	        "NLI evidence — NOT the final D8 adjudication. Treat them as UNVERIFIED-by-D8 pending "
3242	        "a re-judge. See `manifest.json` (`release_disclosure`) for the per-run disclosure "
3243	        "detail.\n\n"
3244	    )

3247	def split_findings_and_limitations(text: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
3248	    """Split a draft into (findings_text, limitations_text).
3249	
3250	    Gap-3: the generator now writes a "Limitations:" paragraph that
3251	    discusses pipeline telemetry (tier mix, contradictions) without
3252	    per-sentence [ev_XXX] markers. Verification should be relaxed for
3253	    that paragraph — no citation tokens are required.
3254	
3255	    Returns ("findings text", "limitations text"). If no Limitations
3256	    block is found, returns (full_text, "").
3257	    """
3258	    if not text:
3259	        return "", ""
3260	    # Find the literal word "Limitations:" at start of a line or after
3261	    # whitespace. Case-insensitive, allow markdown heading variants.
3262	    m = re.search(
3263	        r"(^|\n\s*)(?:#{0,3}\s*)?(?:\*\*)?Limitations(?:\*\*)?\s*:",
3264	        text, re.IGNORECASE,
3265	    )
3266	    if not m:
3267	        return text.strip(), ""
3268	    findings = text[:m.start()].strip()
3269	    limitations = text[m.start():].strip()
3270	    return findings, limitations
3271	
3272	
3273	def verify_limitations_sentence_against_telemetry(
3274	    sentence: str,
3275	    telemetry_block: str,
3276	) -> SentenceVerification:
3277	    """BUG-M-204 fix (deep-dive R10): check that every decimal / integer
3278	    quoted in a Limitations sentence appears verbatim in the telemetry
3279	    block. Sentences that make unsupported numeric claims about the
3280	    pipeline's own corpus are dropped, not passed through.
3281	
3282	    Returns SentenceVerification with is_verified=True/False.
3283	    """
3284	    import re as _re
3285	    tokens = parse_provenance_tokens(sentence)
3286	    # Extract all decimal-or-integer numeric literals from the sentence.
3287	    # Allow percentages; strip '%'.
3288	    sentence_nums = _re.findall(
3289	        r"(?<![A-Za-z\d])-?\d+(?:\.\d+)?(?:%)?", sentence,
3290	    )
3291	    # Only include numbers that are NOT trivial (e.g., "1" in "table 1"
3292	    # isn't a claim; 3+ digit or decimal is more likely a real number).
3293	    substantive = [n for n in sentence_nums if
3294	                   len(n.replace("-", "").replace("%", "").replace(".", "")) >= 2
3295	                   or "." in n or "%" in n]
3296	
3297	    failures: list[str] = []
3298	    if not telemetry_block:
3299	        # No telemetry to check against; treat as pass-through (backward
3300	        # compat when caller doesn't pass the block).
3301	        return SentenceVerification(
3302	            sentence=sentence, tokens=tokens,
3303	            is_verified=True, failure_reasons=[],
3304	            soft_warnings=["limitations_pass_through_no_telemetry"],
3305	        )
3306	    # BUG-M-209 fix (pass 2 remediation): the telemetry block is a
3307	    # whitelist of known metric KEYS. A numeric literal in a Limitations
3308	    # sentence must appear close to a known metric keyword, not just
3309	    # anywhere in telemetry. "T-cell count 500" should NOT pass when
3310	    # telemetry says "http_status: 500".
3311	    #
3312	    # Known telemetry metric anchors, case-insensitive:
3313	    _TELEMETRY_METRIC_KEYS = (
3314	        "tier_distribution",
3315	        "t1", "t2", "t3", "t4", "t5", "t6", "t7",
3316	        "unknown",  # UNKNOWN tier
3317	        "contradictions_detected", "contradictions",
3318	        "rel_diff", "relative_difference",
3319	        "severity",
3320	        "date_range",
3321	        "completeness_gaps", "uncovered", "uncovered_topic",
3322	    )
3323	    telemetry_lower = telemetry_block.lower()
3324	    telemetry_lines = telemetry_lower.split("\n")
3325	
3326	    def _line_mentions_metric(line: str) -> bool:
3327	        for key in _TELEMETRY_METRIC_KEYS:
3328	            if key in line:
3329	                return True
3330	        return False
3331	
3332	    for num in substantive:
3333	        bare = num.rstrip("%")
3334	        bare_escaped = _re.escape(bare)
3335	        num_escaped = _re.escape(num)
3336	        # Check every line that contains the number. The number counts
3337	        # as "backed by telemetry" only if THAT line mentions a known
3338	        # telemetry metric key.
3339	        matched = False
3340	        for line in telemetry_lines:
3341	            has_bare = _re.search(
3342	                rf"(?<![\d.]){bare_escaped}(?![\d])", line,
3343	            )
3344	            has_pct = (num != bare) and _re.search(
3345	                rf"(?<![\d.]){num_escaped}(?![\d])", line,
3346	            )
3347	            if (has_bare or has_pct) and _line_mentions_metric(line):
3348	                matched = True
3349	                break
3350	        if not matched:
3351	            failures.append(f"limitations_number_not_in_telemetry:{num}")
3352	
3353	    if failures:
3354	        return SentenceVerification(
3355	            sentence=sentence, tokens=tokens,
3356	            is_verified=False, failure_reasons=failures,
3357	            soft_warnings=["limitations_paragraph"],
3358	        )
3359	    return SentenceVerification(
3360	        sentence=sentence, tokens=tokens,
3361	        is_verified=True, failure_reasons=[],
3362	        soft_warnings=["limitations_paragraph_verified"],
3363	    )
3364	
3365	
3366	# I-pipe-016 (#1241) — content-empty sentence honesty.
3367	#
3368	# WHY: the lightweight splitter (split_into_sentences) can emit an ORPHANED
3369	# citation-only fragment as its own "sentence" (e.g. a bare `[#ev:a:0-5]` left
3370	# over from a rewrite, or `.[3]` punctuation residue). In the Limitations
3371	# PASS-THROUGH branch these fragments were counted as is_verified=True and
3372	# pulled into BOTH the kept total (numerator) AND total_in (denominator) —
3373	# inflating the verified-sentence telemetry with content-empty noise. (The
3374	# findings branch already drops them via BUG-03 / no_provenance_token, but the
3375	# pass-through branch did not.)
3376	#
3377	# FIX (default-ON, PG_PROVENANCE_SKIP_EMPTY=0 reverts): a content-empty
3378	# "sentence" — no content words AND no numeric values after stripping citation
3379	# artifacts, dose/placebo/threshold residue — is EXCLUDED from the verified
3380	# numerator AND from total_in (denominator). It is neither kept nor dropped; it
3381	# simply does not count. This changes ONLY the counting of empty noise; it does
3382	# NOT alter which REAL sentences pass strict_verify (a real sentence has at
3383	# least one content word or number and is never content-empty).
3384	def _skip_empty_enabled() -> bool:
3385	    """I-pipe-016 (#1241). True (default) => content-empty sentences are
3386	    excluded from the verified numerator AND total_in denominator. Kill-switch:
3387	    PG_PROVENANCE_SKIP_EMPTY=0 reverts to the legacy behavior where a
3388	    content-empty pass-through fragment is counted as verified (byte-identical
3389	    to pre-#1241). Read at call time so tests can toggle without re-import."""
3390	    v = os.getenv("PG_PROVENANCE_SKIP_EMPTY", "1").strip().lower()
3391	    return v in ("1", "true", "yes", "on", "enabled")
3392	
3393	
3394	# fix#19 (#1262) — SPEED, faithfulness-NEUTRAL. The per-sentence entailment judge
3395	# inside verify_sentence_provenance is a BLOCKING LLM call (~tens of seconds each);
3396	# run SERIALLY over hundreds of sentences it is the dominant wall-clock cost of a
3397	# run. PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY opts the findings loop into a BOUNDED thread pool so the
3398	# independent per-sentence judge calls overlap. It changes only WHEN each verdict
3399	# is computed, never WHAT: every sentence still runs the IDENTICAL
3400	# verify_sentence_provenance (+ re-anchor) gate, results are reassembled in the
3401	# ORIGINAL sentence order, and the contextvar run-context (judge telemetry, role,
3402	# provider pin) is COPIED into each worker so no global side effect is lost. The
3403	# hard faithfulness gate is untouched; this is pure scheduling. Default OFF =>
3404	# byte-identical serial path (read at call time so tests can toggle).
3405	#
3406	# Single knob (LAW VI): PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY is the bounded worker count.
3407	#   - unset / 0 / 1 / malformed => OFF => the legacy SERIAL loop runs (byte-identical).
3408	#   - N >= 2                     => parallel with EXACTLY N concurrent judge calls.
3409	# N is a CONCURRENCY bound, never a per-claim TARGET — every sentence is still
3410	# verified; only how many judge calls are in flight at once changes.
3411	def _parallel_verify_workers() -> int:
3412	    """fix#19 (#1262). Resolve PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY to a bounded worker count.
3413	
3414	    Returns 1 (=> serial path, OFF) when unset/empty/0/1/negative/malformed, so the
3415	    default and any non-positive override is byte-identical to the legacy serial
3416	    loop. Returns N>=2 only for an explicit positive override, capping the number
3417	    of concurrent entailment-judge calls. Read at call time so tests can toggle."""
3418	    raw = os.getenv("PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY", "").strip()
3419	    if not raw:
3420	        return 1
3421	    try:
3422	        n = int(raw)
3423	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
3424	        return 1
3425	    return n if n >= 2 else 1
3426	
3427	
3428	def _is_content_empty_sentence(sentence: str) -> bool:
3429	    """I-pipe-016 (#1241). True iff `sentence` carries NO real claim content —
3430	    no content words AND no numeric values — after stripping citation artifacts
3431	    (provenance/calc tokens, atom_NNN, bare [ev_XXX]) and dose/placebo/threshold
3432	    structural residue.
3433	
3434	    Uses the SAME stripping the BUG-03 numeric/empty floor uses inside
3435	    verify_sentence_provenance, so the two definitions agree: a fragment this
3436	    returns True for is exactly the kind verify_sentence_provenance would drop
3437	    with `empty_or_contentless_sentence`. A sentence with ANY content word OR
3438	    ANY number returns False (never excluded), so REAL sentences are untouched.
3439	    """
3440	    cleaned = _verifier_cleaned_text(sentence)
3441	    stripped = _strip_dose_patterns(cleaned)
3442	    stripped = _PLACEBO_COMPARATOR_RE.sub(" ", stripped)
3443	    stripped = _THRESHOLD_RE.sub(" ", stripped)
3444	    no_content = not _content_words(stripped)
3445	    no_numbers = not _decimals_in(stripped) and not _numbers_in(stripped)
3446	    return no_content and no_numbers
3447	
3448	
3449	def _verify_one_findings_sentence(
3450	    s: str,
3451	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
3452	    *,
3453	    require_number_match: bool,
3454	    quantified_models: dict[tuple[str, str], Any] | None,
3455	    reanchor_enabled: bool,
3456	) -> SentenceVerification:
3457	    """fix#19 (#1262): the per-sentence verify-or-rescue decision factored out of
3458	    the strict_verify findings loop so it can be dispatched either serially OR over
3459	    a bounded thread pool WITHOUT changing the outcome.
3460	
3461	    Returns the SINGLE SentenceVerification the loop will keep/drop on: the verified
3462	    result, the re-anchored (rescued) result, or the failed result — caller keeps it
3463	    iff ``.is_verified`` (identical to the legacy inline branch). This is a PURE
3464	    function of its inputs plus the SAME process-global judge/cost side effects the
3465	    inline call already had; it mutates NO shared per-call state (kept/dropped/
3466	    _excluded_empty stay owned by strict_verify), so two sentences can be verified
3467	    concurrently with no interaction. Faithfulness gate logic is byte-for-byte the
3468	    same — only the call SITE moved."""
3469	    v = verify_sentence_provenance(
3470	        s, evidence_pool,
3471	        require_number_match=require_number_match,
3472	        quantified_models=quantified_models,
3473	    )
3474	    if v.is_verified:
3475	        return v
3476	    # I-complete-003 (#1189): before dropping, try to RE-ANCHOR the sentence to a
3477	    # different span in its cited row (or, if uncited but verbatim-grounded, to a
3478	    # pool row). The env gate early-outs so OFF-mode is BYTE-IDENTICAL. A rescued
3479	    # result has already passed the SAME full gate (is_verified=True), so no
3480	    # fabrication path is introduced; the caller keeps it exactly as the inline
3481	    # branch did.
3482	    if reanchor_enabled:
3483	        rescued = _try_reanchor(
3484	            s, evidence_pool,
3485	            require_number_match=require_number_match,
3486	            quantified_models=quantified_models,
3487	        )
3488	        if rescued is not None:
3489	            return rescued
3490	    return v
3491	
3492	
3493	def _pregate_boilerplate_filter_enabled() -> bool:
3494	    """BUG-19 (#1262). True (default) => web-crawl chrome / error-page / pure-
3495	    metadata units are stripped from the section text and excluded from the
3496	    faithfulness gate's INPUT (so they can never be counted as verified claims).
3497	    Kill-switch: PG_PREGATE_STRIP_BOILERPLATE=0 reverts to byte-identical
3498	    pre-#1262 behavior. Read at call time so tests can toggle without re-import."""
3499	    v = os.getenv("PG_PREGATE_STRIP_BOILERPLATE", "1").strip().lower()
3500	    return v in ("1", "true", "yes", "on", "enabled")

3503	def _load_boilerplate_helpers() -> tuple[Any, Any]:
3504	    """BUG-19 (#1262). Lazy-import the allowlist-only boilerplate helpers from
3505	    src.tools.access_bypass so import-time / OFF-path cost is zero (that module
3506	    pulls asyncio/threading/etc.). Returns (strip_web_boilerplate,
3507	    is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional)."""
3508	    from src.tools.access_bypass import (
3509	        is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional,
3510	        strip_web_boilerplate,
3511	    )
3512	    return strip_web_boilerplate, is_boilerplate_or_nonassertional
3513	
3514	
3515	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3516	# I-wire-011 (#1325) fix 5 — chrome/truncation CANARY on the VERIFIED (kept) set.
3517	#
3518	# A faithfulness-STRENGTHENING tripwire: it can only assert that a chrome/truncated
3519	# fragment must NOT be counted VERIFIED — it NEVER promotes a unit and (in the
3520	# default mode) never changes the kept/dropped DECISION. The strict_verify /
3521	# entailment / span-grounding logic is FROZEN. The pregate (`_is_boilerplate`)
3522	# already excludes whole-line chrome from the INPUT, so on a healthy run this is a
3523	# near-no-op; its value is catching what the pregate MISSES (a sentence-form chrome
3524	# span, a mid-word-truncated fetch fragment) before it is rendered as a "verified"
3525	# claim. Env modes (LAW VI, default-safe):
3526	#   off     — disabled (byte-identical).
3527	#   warn    — DEFAULT: detect + LOUD log + telemetry; kept/dropped UNCHANGED (the
3528	#             frozen decision is preserved — render-side screens drop the fragment).
3529	#   enforce — fail-LOUD: raise ChromeReachedVerifiedError so a chrome leak TRIPS the
3530	#             run (opt-in; the run slate may enable it once the render screens hold).
3531	_CHROME_CANARY_ENV = "PG_VERIFY_CHROME_CANARY"
3532	_CHROME_CANARY_TELEMETRY: dict[str, int] = {"chrome_in_kept": 0}
3533	
3534	
3535	class ChromeReachedVerifiedError(RuntimeError):
3536	    """Raised (enforce mode only) when a chrome/truncated unit reached the VERIFIED set."""
3537	
3538	
3539	def _chrome_canary_mode() -> str:
3540	    """Canary mode from ``PG_VERIFY_CHROME_CANARY`` (off|warn|enforce); default ``warn``."""
3541	    mode = os.getenv(_CHROME_CANARY_ENV, "warn").strip().lower()
3542	    return mode if mode in ("off", "warn", "enforce") else "warn"
3543	
3544	
3545	def get_chrome_canary_telemetry() -> dict[str, int]:
3546	    """Snapshot of the chrome-canary hit counter (chrome/truncated units that reached kept)."""
3547	    return dict(_CHROME_CANARY_TELEMETRY)
3548	
3549	
3550	def reset_chrome_canary_telemetry() -> None:
3551	    """Zero the chrome-canary counter (call between runs / tests)."""
3552	    _CHROME_CANARY_TELEMETRY["chrome_in_kept"] = 0
3553	
3554	
3555	def _kept_unit_chrome_reason(sentence: str, is_boilerplate: Any) -> str:
3556	    """The canary reason a VERIFIED unit is chrome/truncated, or "" when clean. PURE-ish
3557	    (lazy import of the high-precision truncation predicate)."""
3558	    if is_boilerplate is not None and is_boilerplate(sentence):
3559	        return "chrome_boilerplate"
3560	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.key_findings import is_truncated_fragment  # noqa: PLC0415
3561	    if is_truncated_fragment(sentence):
3562	        return "truncated_fragment"
3563	    return ""
3564	
3565	
3566	def _run_chrome_canary(kept: list[SentenceVerification]) -> None:
3567	    """Assert no chrome/truncated unit reached the VERIFIED (kept) set.
3568	
3569	    Default ``warn``: log LOUD + bump telemetry, leave kept UNCHANGED (decision frozen). ``enforce``:
3570	    raise ``ChromeReachedVerifiedError``. ``off``: no-op. Loads the boilerplate helpers
3571	    independently of the pregate flag so the canary screens even when the pregate is OFF."""
3572	    mode = _chrome_canary_mode()
3573	    if mode == "off" or not kept:
3574	        return
3575	    try:
3576	        _, _is_boilerplate = _load_boilerplate_helpers()
3577	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — helper import failure must not abort verification
3578	        _is_boilerplate = None
3579	    bad: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
3580	    for sv in kept:
3581	        reason = _kept_unit_chrome_reason(sv.sentence, _is_boilerplate)
3582	        if reason:
3583	            bad.append((sv.sentence, reason))
3584	    if not bad:
3585	        return
3586	    _CHROME_CANARY_TELEMETRY["chrome_in_kept"] += len(bad)
3587	    _summary = "; ".join(f"{r}: {s[:80]!r}" for s, r in bad[:5])
3588	    logger.warning(
3589	        "strict_verify chrome-canary [%s]: %d chrome/truncated unit(s) reached VERIFIED: %s",
3590	        mode, len(bad), _summary,
3591	    )
3592	    if mode == "enforce":
3593	        raise ChromeReachedVerifiedError(
3594	            f"{len(bad)} chrome/truncated unit(s) reached the VERIFIED set: {_summary}"
3595	        )
