3598	def strict_verify(
3599	    draft_text: str,
3600	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
3601	    *,
3602	    require_number_match: bool = True,
3603	    telemetry_block: str | None = None,
3604	    quantified_models: dict[tuple[str, str], Any] | None = None,
3605	) -> StrictVerificationReport:
3606	    """Run strict verification on a draft. Drops failing sentences.
3607	
3608	    Gap-3 (original): the Limitations paragraph was pass-through because
3609	    it discusses pipeline telemetry, not evidence.
3610	
3611	    BUG-M-204 fix (deep-dive R10): when `telemetry_block` is supplied,
3612	    Limitations sentences ARE verified against it — every numeric
3613	    claim in the sentence must appear verbatim in the telemetry block.
3614	    This catches a fabricated "only 3% of sources are T1" claim when
3615	    the telemetry actually says "T1: 9%". Backward-compatible: if
3616	    telemetry_block is None (default), falls back to pass-through.
3617	    """
3618	    findings_text, limitations_text = split_findings_and_limitations(draft_text)
3619	
3620	    kept: list[SentenceVerification] = []
3621	    dropped: list[SentenceVerification] = []
3622	
3623	    # I-pipe-015 (#1240) + I-pipe-016 (#1241) flags, read once per call.
3624	    _honest_tokens = _token_honest_drop_enabled()
3625	    _skip_empty = _skip_empty_enabled()
3626	
3627	    # I-pipe-016 (#1241): content-empty fragments are excluded from total_in
3628	    # (denominator) — they are neither kept nor dropped, they do not count.
3629	    _excluded_empty = 0
3630	
3631	    # BUG-19 (I-arch-006, #1262): pre-gate web-crawl-chrome hygiene. Lazy-load the
3632	    # allowlist-only helpers and strip confirmed crawl-marker LINES from the section
3633	    # text BEFORE sentence-splitting, so a chrome line can't even become a candidate
3634	    # "sentence" (the NTSB 404 "Page not found" hole, where boilerplate trivially
3635	    # self-entails and is COUNTED as verified). `_is_boilerplate` then excludes any
3636	    # surviving pure-chrome / error-page / pure-metadata unit from the gate's INPUT
3637	    # below. INPUT hygiene only — no verdict logic / threshold / gate strictness
3638	    # changes; every pattern is allowlist-only + whole-unit anchored so a real
3639	    # clinical sentence (any language) is never touched. OFF-path (flag=0) is
3640	    # byte-identical: helpers are never loaded, text is untouched.
3641	    _pregate_boilerplate = _pregate_boilerplate_filter_enabled()
3642	    _is_boilerplate = None
3643	    if _pregate_boilerplate:
3644	        _strip_web_boilerplate, _is_boilerplate = _load_boilerplate_helpers()
3645	        findings_text = _strip_web_boilerplate(findings_text)
3646	        limitations_text = _strip_web_boilerplate(limitations_text)
3647	
3648	    # Findings: strict provenance verification
3649	    findings_sentences = split_into_sentences(findings_text)
3650	    # fix#19 (#1262): SEQUENTIAL pre-filter (cheap, deterministic) builds the
3651	    # ORDERED list of canonicalized sentences that need the (expensive) per-sentence
3652	    # judge. It owns _excluded_empty so the denominator semantics are unchanged —
3653	    # only the per-sentence verify-or-rescue call (the blocking entailment judge) is
3654	    # eligible to run in parallel below.
3655	    _findings_to_verify: list[str] = []
3656	    for s in findings_sentences:
3657	        # I-pipe-015 (#1240): canonicalize a malformed `[ev:...]` token to
3658	        # `[#ev:...]` so an otherwise-valid citation is not silently lost. The
3659	        # canonical form is what flows to verify_sentence_provenance (and is
3660	        # stored on .sentence), so downstream token-stripping in
3661	        # resolve_provenance_to_citations works and no malformed literal leaks
3662	        # into the rendered report. The canonicalized token STILL runs the full
3663	        # validation — bracket format is fixed, no check is bypassed.
3664	        if _honest_tokens:
3665	            s = _canonicalize_malformed_ev_tokens(s)
3666	        # I-pipe-016 (#1241): a content-empty fragment (orphaned citation /
3667	        # punctuation residue with no content word and no number) is excluded
3668	        # from BOTH numerator and denominator. verify_sentence_provenance would
3669	        # already DROP it (BUG-03 / no_provenance_token), so excluding it from
3670	        # the denominator instead of dropping it only removes empty noise from
3671	        # the telemetry; it never lets an unverified REAL sentence through.
3672	        if _skip_empty and _is_content_empty_sentence(s):
3673	            _excluded_empty += 1
3674	            continue
3675	        # BUG-19 (#1262): a pure web-crawl-chrome / error-page-stub / bare-DOI /
3676	        # table-number "sentence" carries NO assertional claim — it must NOT reach
3677	        # the gate, where boilerplate would trivially self-entail and be COUNTED as
3678	        # verified (the NTSB 404 "Page not found" hole). Exclude it from BOTH
3679	        # numerator and denominator (same faithfulness-neutral treatment as the
3680	        # content-empty path). INPUT hygiene only: the helper is allowlist-only +
3681	        # whole-unit anchored, so a real clinical sentence is never flagged.
3682	        if _is_boilerplate is not None and _is_boilerplate(s):
3683	            _excluded_empty += 1
3684	            continue
3685	        _findings_to_verify.append(s)
3686	
3687	    # fix#19 (#1262): verify each surviving sentence — the SAME verify-or-rescue
3688	    # decision as before, just dispatched either serially (default) or across a
3689	    # BOUNDED thread pool. Verdicts are independent; results are reassembled in the
3690	    # ORIGINAL order so kept/dropped are byte-identical to the serial path.
3691	    _reanchor_enabled = _provenance_reanchor_enabled()
3692	    _verify_workers = _parallel_verify_workers()
3693	    if _verify_workers < 2 or len(_findings_to_verify) < 2:
3694	        # Serial path (default / single-item): byte-identical to the legacy loop.
3695	        _findings_results = [
3696	            _verify_one_findings_sentence(
3697	                s, evidence_pool,
3698	                require_number_match=require_number_match,
3699	                quantified_models=quantified_models,
3700	                reanchor_enabled=_reanchor_enabled,
3701	            )
3702	            for s in _findings_to_verify
3703	        ]
3704	    else:
3705	        # Parallel path: a BOUNDED ThreadPoolExecutor caps concurrency at exactly
3706	        # _verify_workers. A fresh worker thread starts with an EMPTY contextvars
3707	        # context, which would silently drop the per-run judge telemetry (FX-09
3708	        # ContextVar holding the run's {calls, judge_error} dict), the ambient role,
3709	        # and the gate's provider pin. So we capture the PARENT context ONCE here in
3710	        # the calling thread and run each task inside a copy of it — the ContextVar
3711	        # still references the SAME run-telemetry dict object, so the in-place
3712	        # ``_run_tel[...] += 1`` ticks land in the right counter. ``map`` preserves
3713	        # input order => the reassembled list is index-aligned with
3714	        # _findings_to_verify (deterministic kept/dropped, never race-ordered). A
3715	        # worker exception PROPAGATES out of map() (fail-loud — a hard gate /
3716	        # programming defect is never swallowed), exactly as the serial loop raises.
3717	        import concurrent.futures as _futures  # noqa: PLC0415 (lazy: zero OFF-path cost)
3718	        import contextvars as _ctxvars  # noqa: PLC0415
3719	
3720	        # I-arch-011 (Codex FIX-C P1): each worker runs in a COPIED context, so the entailment
3721	        # judge's ``_orc._add_run_cost`` / ``check_run_budget`` mutate the COPY's ``_RUN_COST_CTX``
3722	        # (lost to the parent) — the parallel verify spend would bypass PG_MAX_COST_PER_RUN and the
3723	        # run-budget gate. The cost LEDGER stays accurate (``append_cost_ledger_row`` bumps a
3724	        # process-global, lock-protected per-session accumulator, NOT a contextvar). Mirror the
3725	        # credibility_pass offload reconciliation (openrouter_client.ledger_cumulative docstring):
3726	        # snapshot the per-session ledger cumulative before/after the pool and re-add the delta to the
3727	        # PARENT ``_RUN_COST_CTX``, then re-check the budget so the gate is inclusive of the parallel
3728	        # judge spend. Faithfulness-NEUTRAL (cost accounting only; verdicts unchanged).
3729	        from src.polaris_graph.llm import openrouter_client as _orc_cost  # noqa: PLC0415
3730	        _run_id = _orc_cost._CURRENT_RUN_ID_CTX.get()
3731	        _cost_before = _orc_cost.ledger_cumulative(_run_id)
3732	
3733	        _parent_ctx = _ctxvars.copy_context()
3734	
3735	        def _verify_in_context(_s: str) -> SentenceVerification:
3736	            return _parent_ctx.copy().run(
3737	                _verify_one_findings_sentence,
3738	                _s, evidence_pool,
3739	                require_number_match=require_number_match,
3740	                quantified_models=quantified_models,
3741	                reanchor_enabled=_reanchor_enabled,
3742	            )
3743	
3744	        with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_verify_workers) as _pool:
3745	            _findings_results = list(_pool.map(_verify_in_context, _findings_to_verify))
3746	
3747	        # Reconcile the parallel verify spend into the parent budget gate (see note above).
3748	        _cost_delta = _orc_cost.ledger_cumulative(_run_id) - _cost_before
3749	        if _cost_delta > 0:
3750	            _orc_cost._add_run_cost(_cost_delta)
3751	            _orc_cost.check_run_budget(0)  # raises BudgetExceededError if the pool breached the cap
3752	
3753	    for v in _findings_results:
3754	        # Keep iff verified (a re-anchored rescue is returned already is_verified=
3755	        # True), drop otherwise — identical decision to the legacy inline branch.
3756	        if v.is_verified:
3757	            kept.append(v)
3758	        else:
3759	            dropped.append(v)
3760	
3761	    # Limitations: telemetry-grounded verification if block supplied,
3762	    # else pass-through (M-204 backward-compat).
3763	    limitations_sentences = split_into_sentences(limitations_text)
3764	    for s in limitations_sentences:
3765	        # I-pipe-015 (#1240): same malformed-token canonicalization as findings.
3766	        if _honest_tokens:
3767	            s = _canonicalize_malformed_ev_tokens(s)
3768	        # I-pipe-016 (#1241): exclude content-empty pass-through fragments from
3769	        # the verified numerator AND total_in. This is the PRIMARY inflation
3770	        # site the forensic flagged: the pass-through branch below counted a
3771	        # bare `[#ev:...]` orphan as is_verified=True. A REAL limitations
3772	        # sentence carries telemetry numbers / words and is never excluded.
3773	        if _skip_empty and _is_content_empty_sentence(s):
3774	            _excluded_empty += 1
3775	            continue
3776	        # BUG-19 (#1262): exclude pure web-crawl-chrome / error-page / pure-metadata
3777	        # units from the limitations branch too — its pass-through path (below)
3778	        # counts a unit as is_verified=True, so a chrome line reaching it would be
3779	        # COUNTED as a verified claim. Same allowlist-only, whole-unit treatment as
3780	        # findings; a real telemetry sentence is never flagged.
3781	        if _is_boilerplate is not None and _is_boilerplate(s):
3782	            _excluded_empty += 1
3783	            continue
3784	        if telemetry_block is not None:
3785	            v = verify_limitations_sentence_against_telemetry(
3786	                s, telemetry_block,
3787	            )
3788	            if v.is_verified:
3789	                kept.append(v)
3790	            else:
3791	                dropped.append(v)
3792	        else:
3793	            tokens = parse_provenance_tokens(s)
3794	            kept.append(SentenceVerification(
3795	                sentence=s,
3796	                tokens=tokens,
3797	                is_verified=True,
3798	                failure_reasons=[],
3799	                soft_warnings=["limitations_paragraph_pass_through"],
3800	            ))
3801	
3802	    # I-wire-011 (#1325) fix 5: chrome/truncation canary on the VERIFIED set. Default ``warn`` logs
3803	    # + counts and leaves the kept/dropped decision FROZEN; ``enforce`` raises. Strengthening only.
3804	    _run_chrome_canary(kept)
3805	
3806	    # I-pipe-016 (#1241): total_in excludes content-empty fragments so the
3807	    # verified ratio reflects only real sentences. When PG_PROVENANCE_SKIP_EMPTY
3808	    # is OFF, _excluded_empty stays 0 and total_in is byte-identical to before.
3809	    total_in = (
3810	        len(findings_sentences) + len(limitations_sentences) - _excluded_empty
3811	    )
3812	    return StrictVerificationReport(
3813	        kept_sentences=kept,
3814	        dropped_sentences=dropped,
3815	        total_in=total_in,
3816	        total_kept=len(kept),
3817	        total_dropped=len(dropped),

3826	def _basket_for_biblio(basket: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
3827	    """Project a ``credibility_pass.ClaimBasket`` onto the bibliography render
3828	    contract (design §6, checklist [11] P5.2).
3829	
3830	    Surfaces ONLY what the basket already computed upstream — never recomputes a
3831	    verdict and never resurrects a dropped sentence (the basket is assembled in
3832	    ``credibility_pass`` AFTER strict_verify; a dropped sentence never reaches
3833	    this resolver, so a ``basket_verdict=full`` can't upgrade it). Each member is
3834	    shown with its OWN isolated ``span_verdict``: a member with no verified span
3835	    is surfaced as context/unverified, never silently counted as support. The
3836	    basket itself is labelled ``partial``/``contested`` in that case (never
3837	    silently ``full``) by the upstream assembly.
3838	
3839	    Read via ``getattr`` (duck-typed) so this module never imports
3840	    ``credibility_pass`` (which lazy-imports THIS module — a hard cycle).
3841	    """
3842	    members_out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
3843	    for member in (getattr(basket, "supporting_members", None) or []):
3844	        members_out.append({
3845	            "evidence_id": str(getattr(member, "evidence_id", "") or ""),
3846	            "source_url": str(getattr(member, "source_url", "") or ""),
3847	            "source_tier": str(getattr(member, "source_tier", "") or ""),
3848	            "origin_cluster_id": str(getattr(member, "origin_cluster_id", "") or ""),
3849	            "credibility_weight": getattr(member, "credibility_weight", None),
3850	            "authority_score": float(getattr(member, "authority_score", 0.0) or 0.0),
3851	            # the member's OWN isolated span verdict — SUPPORTS members are the
3852	            # ones counted in verified_support_origin_count; UNSUPPORTED members
3853	            # are shown as context, never as support strength.
3854	            "span_verdict": str(getattr(member, "span_verdict", "") or ""),
3855	            # I-arch-010 FIX-2 Step 0 — the additive 3-value entailment tier. Carried
3856	            # through the projection so the I-arch-011 keep-with-labels layer can read it;
3857	            # the render/count consumers below key ONLY on span_verdict (byte-unchanged).
3858	            "member_tier": str(getattr(member, "member_tier", "") or ""),
3859	            "direct_quote": str(getattr(member, "direct_quote", "") or ""),
3860	        })
3861	    refuter_ids = tuple(
3862	        str(c) for c in (getattr(basket, "refuter_cluster_ids", ()) or ())
3863	    )
3864	    return {
3865	        "claim_cluster_id": str(getattr(basket, "claim_cluster_id", "") or ""),
3866	        "claim_text": str(getattr(basket, "claim_text", "") or ""),
3867	        "subject": str(getattr(basket, "subject", "") or ""),
3868	        "predicate": str(getattr(basket, "predicate", "") or ""),
3869	        # the ONLY strengthening count — N VERIFIED independent origins (isolated
3870	        # per-member verification), NEVER the clustered total.
3871	        "verified_support_origin_count": int(
3872	            getattr(basket, "verified_support_origin_count", 0) or 0
3873	        ),
3874	        # ADVISORY clustered count — surfaced labelled, never as support strength.
3875	        "total_clustered_origin_count": int(
3876	            getattr(basket, "total_clustered_origin_count", 0) or 0
3877	        ),
3878	        "weight_mass": float(getattr(basket, "weight_mass", 0.0) or 0.0),
3879	        "basket_verdict": str(getattr(basket, "basket_verdict", "") or ""),
3880	        # contested -> reference to the both_sides neutral block (the refuting
3881	        # cluster ids), never the refuters' content duplicated here.
3882	        "refuter_cluster_ids": refuter_ids,
3883	        "supporting_members": members_out,
3884	    }

3887	def build_basket_supports_by_cluster(
3888	    basket_by_cluster: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
3889	) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
3890	    """Index claim_cluster_id -> the list of evidence_ids whose OWN isolated
3891	    ``span_verdict == "SUPPORTS"`` (I-arch-005 B6/B8 #1257, the keystone).
3892	
3893	    Input is the per-cluster PROJECTED basket dict map (``_basket_for_biblio``
3894	    output keyed by ``claim_cluster_id``). Output keeps ONLY the
3895	    independently span-verified (SUPPORTS) members — the same members counted in
3896	    ``verified_support_origin_count``, NEVER the advisory
3897	    ``total_clustered_origin_count``. A member with no verified span is skipped
3898	    (it is shown only in the bibliography as context, never rendered as inline
3899	    support). Module-level so BOTH the legacy resolver AND the V30 contract
3900	    slot-regroup (contract_section_runner.py) compute corroborators from the
3901	    SAME faithfulness-critical logic — no second copy to drift.
3902	    """
3903	    out: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
3904	    for _ccid, _bdict in basket_by_cluster.items():
3905	        _supports = [
3906	            str(m.get("evidence_id") or "")
3907	            for m in (_bdict.get("supporting_members") or [])
3908	            if str(m.get("span_verdict") or "").upper() == "SUPPORTS"
3909	            and str(m.get("evidence_id") or "")
3910	        ]
3911	        if _supports:
3912	            out[_ccid] = _supports
3913	    return out
3914	
3915	
3916	def verified_corroborators_with_clusters_for_tokens(
3917	    token_ev_ids: list[str],
3918	    *,
3919	    basket_supports_by_cluster: dict[str, list[str]],
3920	    cluster_id_by_evidence: dict[str, list[str]] | None,
3921	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
3922	) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
3923	    """Like ``verified_corroborators_for_tokens`` but ALSO returns, per corroborator,
3924	    the SELECTED claim cluster it was pulled in through — the own-token's single
3925	    ``claim_cluster_id``. Order is deterministic (token order, then member order); a
3926	    corroborator eid is emitted once (first own-token that pulls it in wins, via the
3927	    ``seen`` dedup), and its paired cluster is THAT own-token's cluster.
3928	
3929	    I-beatboth-011 (#1289) P1 (multi-cluster span): a corroborator eid can be a SUPPORTS
3930	    member of SEVERAL clusters with DIFFERENT claim-local ``direct_quote`` spans. The
3931	    grounding filter must read the span of the cluster the corroborator was ACTUALLY
3932	    selected through (this own-token's single cluster ``_ccids[0]``) — NOT a global
3933	    first-match across all clusters, which could read a sibling cluster's (wrong) span and
3934	    so drop a true grounder or keep a wrong one. This sibling exposes that selected cluster
3935	    so the caller can resolve the RIGHT claim-local span.
3936	    """
3937	    if not basket_supports_by_cluster:
3938	        return []
3939	    seen: set[str] = set()
3940	    out: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
3941	    for _eid in token_ev_ids:
3942	        _ccids = (cluster_id_by_evidence or {}).get(_eid, []) or []
3943	        # Anti-cross-claim: only an UNAMBIGUOUS single-cluster token may corroborate
3944	        # (a multi-cluster token can't be attributed to ONE claim -> skip its expansion).
3945	        if len(_ccids) != 1:
3946	            continue
3947	        _selected_cluster = _ccids[0]
3948	        for _support_eid in basket_supports_by_cluster.get(_selected_cluster, []):
3949	            if _support_eid not in seen and _support_eid in evidence_pool:
3950	                seen.add(_support_eid)
3951	                out.append((_support_eid, _selected_cluster))
3952	    return out
3953	
3954	
3955	def verified_corroborators_for_tokens(
3956	    token_ev_ids: list[str],
3957	    *,
3958	    basket_supports_by_cluster: dict[str, list[str]],
3959	    cluster_id_by_evidence: dict[str, list[str]] | None,
3960	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
3961	) -> list[str]:
3962	    """Union of independently SPAN-VERIFIED (SUPPORTS) basket members backing the
3963	    claim the sentence's OWN cited tokens map to (I-arch-005 B6/B8 #1257). Joins
3964	    via the sentence's own evidence_id -> ``cluster_id_by_evidence`` -> basket
3965	    SUPPORTS members (NOT a fuzzy claim match). Only members resolvable in
3966	    ``evidence_pool`` are returned. Order is deterministic (token order, then
3967	    member order). Returns [] when basket data is absent => the OFF path is
3968	    byte-identical.
3969	
3970	    FAITHFULNESS — anti-cross-claim attribution (constraint 1, §-1.1 "citation
3971	    appropriate for the claim"): ``cluster_id_by_evidence`` is 1-to-MANY (one
3972	    source can back several DISTINCT claims, e.g. ev_a backs "reduces weight 14%"
3973	    AND "causes nausea 10%"). A cited token does NOT tell us WHICH of the source's
3974	    claims the SENTENCE asserts, so expanding a MULTI-cluster token would risk
3975	    rendering cluster-2's verified member as support for a cluster-1 sentence — a
3976	    wrong-claim citation (lethal in clinical context). We therefore expand ONLY a
3977	    token whose evidence_id maps to EXACTLY ONE cluster (the sentence's claim is
3978	    then unambiguous); a multi-cluster token keeps its own single citation, never
3979	    an inferred cross-claim corroborator. Conservative on purpose: faithfulness
3980	    over completeness.
3981	
3982	    Module-level (extracted from the resolver's former nested closure) so the V30
3983	    contract slot-regroup uses the IDENTICAL logic — single source of truth for the
3984	    keystone's faithfulness rules. Thin wrapper over
3985	    ``verified_corroborators_with_clusters_for_tokens`` (the return shape stays
3986	    ``list[str]`` so the un-editable contract caller keeps compiling byte-identically).
3987	    """
3988	    return [
3989	        _eid
3990	        for _eid, _ccid in verified_corroborators_with_clusters_for_tokens(
3991	            token_ev_ids,
3992	            basket_supports_by_cluster=basket_supports_by_cluster,
3993	            cluster_id_by_evidence=cluster_id_by_evidence,
3994	            evidence_pool=evidence_pool,
3995	        )
3996	    ]

3999	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4000	# I-deepfix-001 P2 (#1344) — CITATION-PURITY directional-NLI co-support gate.
4001	#
4002	# The lexical/numeric grounding predicate (``corroborator_span_grounds_sentence``) can still
4003	# clear a corroborator whose span shares only GENERIC vocabulary with the claim (the residual
4004	# noted in that function's docstring: a wrong automation source sharing
4005	# "change"/"employment"/"occupations"). P2 STACKS a directional NLI check BENEATH that floor —
4006	# an AND, so it can ONLY ever TIGHTEN (detach an off-topic [N]); it never keeps a corroborator
4007	# the lexical/numeric floor already dropped. The direction is ALCE / DeepTRACE: the cited SPAN
4008	# (premise) must entail the CLAIM (hypothesis). The hypothesis is CLAIM-LOCAL — decomposed into
4009	# clause spans — so a genuine corroborator of ONE clause in a multi-clause sentence is NOT
4010	# stripped because ANOTHER clause is about a different figure (ALCE per-statement precision,
4011	# 2305.14627). Reuses the ALREADY-LOADED consolidation cross-encoder => ZERO extra spend.
4012	#
4013	# DNA (§-1.3): this is a WEIGHT/PURITY DETACH — the source STAYS in the bibliography; only its
4014	# INLINE [N] is withheld for a sentence its span does not entail. Never a source-type drop
4015	# (WEF / OECD / gov keep full weight); the ONLY thing removed is a citation number that does
4016	# not fit THIS claim. Env-gated ``PG_CITATION_NLI_PURITY``; the DEFAULT is OFF (=byte-identical
4017	# legacy render, no cross-encoder call) and the run slate turns it ON (=1) for the paid run.
4018	#
4019	# WHY DEFAULT-OFF (not the fix-card's "default-ON"): the SAME master plan STEP-7 requires the
4020	# nli_detached RATE + withheld_own_token COUNT to be calibrated on a FRESH scored run BEFORE
4021	# default-ON is TRUSTED ("If ... anomalously high, HOLD default-ON and recalibrate"). Empirically
4022	# the cross-encoder over-detaches on SHORT synthetic spans (it stripped legitimate corroborators
4023	# from the existing render fixtures when this shipped default-ON), so shipping OFF-by-default is
4024	# the faithful, §8.4-safe (no cross-encoder in every CI render) reading — the flag is flipped ON
4025	# in the run slate and calibration-gated per STEP-7, exactly how PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI /
4026	# PG_RELEVANCE_GATE ship. ``=1`` = the P2 gate active; unset/``=0`` = byte-identical revert.
4027	#
4028	# ORDERING vs PG_RELEVANCE_GATE: the NLI purity runs INSIDE the shared grounding predicate
4029	# (``corroborator_grounds_sentence_via_basket``), so it is applied UPSTREAM of the SURE-RAG
4030	# relevance gate — a corroborator must pass lexical/numeric AND NLI-purity to be GROUNDED, and
4031	# only THEN is it (separately) exposed to any PG_RELEVANCE_GATE demotion. The two layers
4032	# compose (both can only tighten); neither depends on the other's flag.
4033	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4034	
4035	# A single constant so the operator can flip the ship default without touching call sites.
4036	# Read at call time (so a harness / the run slate can toggle per-case). LAW VI: env-driven.
4037	_CITATION_NLI_PURITY_DEFAULT = "0"
4038	
4039	
4040	def _citation_nli_purity_enabled() -> bool:
4041	    """``PG_CITATION_NLI_PURITY`` gate (default-OFF; the run slate sets =1). OFF => the NLI
4042	    co-support AND-stack and the render-time own-token bounds re-assert are BOTH skipped =>
4043	    byte-identical legacy render (no cross-encoder call)."""
4044	    return os.getenv("PG_CITATION_NLI_PURITY", _CITATION_NLI_PURITY_DEFAULT).strip().lower() \
4045	        not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
4046	
4047	
4048	# Telemetry: per-process counters so a run can PROVE the purity gate FIRED (and how much it
4049	# detached / withheld) in the output — the §-1.4 fired-not-configured assertion, and the
4050	# STEP-7 fresh-run calibration rows (nli_detached RATE, withheld_own_token count).
4051	_PURITY_TELEMETRY: dict[str, int] = {
4052	    "nli_detached": 0,               # corroborator [N] detached by the NLI co-support gate
4053	    "nli_kept": 0,                   # corroborator kept on a CONFIRMED clause entailment
4054	    "nli_unknown_kept": 0,           # corroborator kept because the NLI verdict was UNAVAILABLE
4055	    "withheld_own_token": 0,         # own-token [N] withheld by the render-time bounds re-assert
4056	    "own_token_retention_kept": 0,   # own-token withhold BLOCKED by the min-retention guard
4057	}
4058

4060	def get_purity_telemetry() -> dict[str, int]:
4061	    """Snapshot of the P2 citation-purity counters (for the §-1.4 fired-in-output assertion
4062	    and the STEP-7 fresh-run calibration acceptance rows)."""
4063	    return dict(_PURITY_TELEMETRY)

4065	
4066	def reset_purity_telemetry() -> None:
4067	    for _k in _PURITY_TELEMETRY:
4068	        _PURITY_TELEMETRY[_k] = 0

4070	
4071	# Clause-boundary splitter for the CLAIM-LOCAL hypothesis (ALCE per-statement, 2305.14627).
4072	# Splits on clause punctuation + coordinating/subordinating connectors so each clause of a
4073	# compound sentence becomes its own hypothesis. Conservative: over-splitting only makes the
4074	# gate KEEP more (an easier hypothesis to entail), never manufactures a false detach.
4075	_CLAIM_CLAUSE_SPLIT_RE = re.compile(
4076	    r"[;,]|\b(?:and|or|but|while|whereas|although|though|however|which|that)\b",
4077	    re.IGNORECASE,
4078	)
4079	
4080	
4081	def _claim_local_hypotheses(claim: str) -> list[str]:
4082	    """Decompose ``claim`` into claim-local clause hypotheses. A corroborator counts as
4083	    supporting if its span entails ANY clause (partial-support). Full-sentence entailment is
4084	    the FALLBACK only when no clause decomposition is available (a single-clause sentence)."""
4085	    if not claim or not claim.strip():
4086	        return []
4087	    parts = [p.strip() for p in _CLAIM_CLAUSE_SPLIT_RE.split(claim) if p and p.strip()]
4088	    # Keep only clauses with real content (>=1 content word) so a bare connector fragment
4089	    # cannot become a trivially-entailed hypothesis.
4090	    clauses = [c for c in parts if _content_words(c)]
4091	    return clauses if clauses else [claim.strip()]
4092	
4093	
4094	def _corroborator_nli_co_supports(claim: str, span: str) -> Optional[bool]:
4095	    """Directional NLI co-support: does the corroborator ``span`` (premise) entail ANY
4096	    claim-local clause of ``claim`` (hypothesis)? ALCE / DeepTRACE direction.
4097	
4098	    Returns ``True`` (a clause is entailed => keep), ``False`` (a CONFIDENT non-entailment on
4099	    EVERY clause => detach), or ``None`` (verdict unavailable on at least one clause and none
4100	    entailed => keep on the already-passed lexical/numeric grounding). We detach ONLY on a
4101	    confident all-clauses-non-entail verdict, so an infra fault (model down) can never strip a
4102	    real corroborator (§-1.3 breadth-safe)."""

4118	def corroborator_span_grounds_sentence(
4119	    sentence_claim: str,
4120	    corroborator_span: str,
4121	    *,
4122	    min_content_overlap: int = MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP,
4123	) -> bool:
4124	    """Does ``corroborator_span`` actually carry THIS sentence's claim?
4125	
4126	    I-beatboth-011 (#1289) — anti-mis-attribution corroborator filter (defect #6).
4127	    ``verified_corroborators_for_tokens`` attaches a basket member's inline ``[N]`` to a
4128	    sentence by CLUSTER MEMBERSHIP (the member's OWN span SUPPORTS the cluster claim), NOT by
4129	    whether that member's span grounds the SPECIFIC sentence being rendered. Within one
4130	    cluster, sentence S1 (cited via own-token ev_A) and sentence S2 (cited via own-token
4131	    ev_B) both pull in EVERY SUPPORTS member as corroborators — so ev_B's ``[N]`` glues onto
4132	    S1 even though ev_B's span never carries S1's exact assertion. Observed in
4133	    ``drb_72_ai_labor``: [85] (a Mapping-AI page-header span) glued to a BLS sentence only
4134	    [84] grounds; [17]/[19] (World-Bank / EPI spans lacking "displacement") glued to a
4135	    displacement sentence only [18] grounds.
4136	
4137	    This applies the SAME content-word-overlap predicate ``strict_verify`` (invariant #3)
4138	    already uses for own-token grounding — a READ of that predicate's logic via the shared
4139	    module-level ``_content_words`` helper, NOT a change to the faithfulness engine. A
4140	    corroborator is kept ONLY if its span shares >= ``min_content_overlap`` distinctive
4141	    content words with the sentence's claim. This TIGHTENS faithfulness (removes UNSUPPORTED
4142	    cross-claim attributions); it can never relax it.
4143	
4144	    TWO sufficient grounding paths (mirroring how strict_verify recognises grounding, but
4145	    LOOSER on lexical overlap because an INDEPENDENT corroborator legitimately paraphrases the
4146	    same fact with fewer shared words than a generated own-token, which is drawn FROM its span):
4147	
4148	      * NUMERIC corroboration — EVERY decimal figure in the claim also appears in the span
4149	        (the same "every decimal in sentence appears in span" rule strict_verify invariant #3
4150	        uses, via ``_decimals_in``) AND the span shares >= 1 distinctive content word with the
4151	        claim. A corroborator that independently reports the SAME figure (e.g. "14.9%") is
4152	        genuine multi-citation even when it paraphrases the surrounding prose; the >=1 word
4153	        guard blocks a bare-number coincidence.
4154	      * LEXICAL corroboration — the span shares >= ``min_content_overlap`` distinctive content
4155	        words with the claim (the content-word path for qualitative claims).
4156	
4157	    A corroborator is KEPT if EITHER path holds; it is dropped only when its span neither
4158	    carries the claim's figures nor shares its distinctive vocabulary. This TIGHTENS
4159	    faithfulness (removes UNSUPPORTED cross-claim attributions) while preserving genuine
4160	    multi-citation (§-1.3). NOTE (residual, surfaced in coordination): a member whose span
4161	    shares only GENERIC vocabulary (e.g. a wrong automation source sharing
4162	    "change"/"employment"/"occupations") can still clear a purely-mechanical overlap floor —
4163	    that residual is what the LLM relevance gate (``PG_RELEVANCE_GATE``, lines below) catches;
4164	    this filter STACKS beneath it at zero spend.
4165	    """
4166	    if not sentence_claim or not corroborator_span:
4167	        # No claim text or no span to compare => cannot affirm grounding. Conservative:
4168	        # an empty span carries no claim, so it must not be attached as inline support.
4169	        return False
4170	    _claim_words = _content_words(sentence_claim)
4171	    _span_words = _content_words(corroborator_span)
4172	    _overlap = len(_claim_words & _span_words)
4173	    if _overlap >= min_content_overlap:
4174	        return True  # lexical corroboration (qualitative claim)
4175	    # I-deepfix-001 (Wave-2) PERCENT-ROLE gate on the NUMERIC corroboration paths below.
4176	    # A corroborator that grounds a claim on FIGURE-subset alone (< min_content_overlap shared
4177	    # words) must carry every PERCENT the claim prints AS A PERCENT — not merely as a bare digit
4178	    # (a page number / year / plain count) that happens to equal the percent value. Compare
4179	    # PERCENT-vs-PERCENT via ``_percents_in`` (same ``_INTEGER_PERCENT_RE`` on both sides). This
4180	    # runs ONLY after the lexical path has already returned (a >=2-word qualitative corroborator
4181	    # is untouched), so it is STRICTLY ADDITIVE: it can only DETACH a numeric-only corroborator
4182	    # whose span lacks the claim's printed percent, never keep one it drops today. Default-ON;
4183	    # PG_PROVENANCE_PERCENT_ROLE_MATCH=0 reverts byte-identical.
4184	    if _percent_role_match_enabled():
4185	        _claim_pcts = _percents_in(sentence_claim)
4186	        if _claim_pcts and not _claim_pcts.issubset(_percents_in(corroborator_span)):
4187	            return False
4188	    # Numeric corroboration: the span independently carries every figure the claim asserts AND
4189	    # shares at least one distinctive content word (so it is the SAME claim, not a coincidental
4190	    # number match — the >=1 guard blocks "50% of X" matching any span that merely contains
4191	    # "50"). Mirrors strict_verify's numeric branching (invariant #3, lines ~1969-2002): the
4192	    # DECIMAL branch additionally checks %-expressed integers, and a claim with NO decimal is
4193	    # carried by its standalone INTEGERS. I-beatboth-011 P1#4: the prior path checked ONLY
4194	    # ``_decimals_in`` => a genuine corroborator for an integer-percentage ("50%"/"19%") or a
4195	    # plain integer claim was false-dropped unless it ALSO shared two lexical content words,
4196	    # losing real multi-citation. We now READ the SAME ``_INTEGER_PERCENT_RE`` / ``_numbers_in``
4197	    # predicates strict_verify uses for own tokens (no engine change), so integer-% / integer-
4198	    # only corroboration is preserved with the identical figure-in-span rule.
4199	    _span_decimals = _decimals_in(corroborator_span)
4200	    _span_numbers = _numbers_in(corroborator_span)

content.content:
4201	    _span_int_only = _span_numbers - _span_decimals
4202	    _claim_decimals = _decimals_in(sentence_claim)
4203	    if _claim_decimals:
4204	        if not _claim_decimals.issubset(_span_decimals):
4205	            return False
4206	        # The decimal(s) are carried. A %-expressed integer beside the decimal (strict_verify
4207	        # ``_INTEGER_PERCENT_RE`` minus decimals) is ALSO part of the claim — require it in the
4208	        # span's integer-only set too (mirrors strict_verify lines ~1981-1991).
4209	        _claim_pct_ints = {
4210	            m.group(1) for m in _INTEGER_PERCENT_RE.finditer(sentence_claim)
4211	        } - _claim_decimals
4212	        if _claim_pct_ints and not _claim_pct_ints.issubset(_span_int_only):
4213	            return False
4214	        return _overlap >= 1
4215	    # No decimals: the INTEGERS are the claim. I-beatboth-011 P1 (plain integer-only): mirror
4216	    # strict_verify's no-decimal branch EXACTLY (lines ~1992-2002) — there EVERY standalone
4217	    # integer the claim asserts (``_numbers_in(sentence_stripped)``) must appear in a cited
4218	    # span's number set (``aggregated_span_numbers``). The prior corroborator path checked ONLY
4219	    # the %-expressed integers (``_INTEGER_PERCENT_RE``), so a TRUE grounding corroborator for a
4220	    # PLAIN integer-only claim (e.g. "5,172 agents were tested", "47 occupations") was wrongly
4221	    # DETACHED whenever its lexical overlap was below the 2-word floor — even when ALL the
4222	    # asserted integers ARE present in its span. We now READ the SAME ``_numbers_in`` predicate
4223	    # strict_verify uses (no engine change): if the claim asserts integers and the span's number
4224	    # set contains ALL of them, that grounds it (independent of lexical overlap), with the >=1
4225	    # content-word coincidence guard retained so a bare-number match is never enough.
4226	    _claim_numbers = _numbers_in(sentence_claim)
4227	    if _claim_numbers and _claim_numbers.issubset(_span_numbers):
4228	        return _overlap >= 1
4229	    # Fallback: a claim whose ONLY figures are %-expressed integers (the prior path). Kept for
4230	    # parity with the strict_verify decimal-branch %-integer check; ``_numbers_in`` above already
4231	    # subsumes the standalone-integer case, so this only fires when ``_numbers_in`` is empty yet a
4232	    # %-expressed integer survives (defensive — same >=1 coincidence guard).
4233	    _claim_pct_ints = {
4234	        m.group(1) for m in _INTEGER_PERCENT_RE.finditer(sentence_claim)
4235	    }
4236	    if _claim_pct_ints and _claim_pct_ints.issubset(_span_int_only):
4237	        return _overlap >= 1
4238	    return False

4241	def _claim_local_corroborator_span(
4242	    corroborator_eid: str,
4243	    basket_by_cluster: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
4244	    *,
4245	    selected_cluster_id: str | None = None,
4246	) -> str:
4247	    """The basket member's CLAIM-LOCAL supporting span (its ``direct_quote``) for the
4248	    given corroborator evidence_id.
4249	
4250	    I-beatboth-011 (#1289) P1#2: the anti-mis-attribution filter must ground a corroborator
4251	    against the SPAN THE BASKET STORED for that member — ``BasketMember.direct_quote``, the
4252	    claim-local cited span set by ``credibility_pass`` (and preserved verbatim through
4253	    ``_basket_for_biblio`` at the projection above) — NOT the broad ``evidence_pool`` row text
4254	    (which can be the whole page/row and would let a cross-claim span clear the overlap floor).
4255	    Returns "" when the eid is in no projected basket member — the caller treats an
4256	    empty span as ungrounded (conservative drop), never as a row-text fallback.
4257	
4258	    I-beatboth-011 (#1289) P1 (multi-cluster span): a corroborator eid can be a SUPPORTS member
4259	    of SEVERAL clusters with DIFFERENT claim-local spans. ``selected_cluster_id`` is the cluster
4260	    the corroborator was ACTUALLY selected through (``verified_corroborators_with_clusters_for_tokens``
4261	    pairs each corroborator with its own-token's single cluster). When supplied, resolve the span
4262	    from THAT cluster's member ONLY — so the grounding check reads the RIGHT claim-local span for
4263	    the selected cluster, never a sibling cluster's (which would drop a true grounder or keep a
4264	    wrong one). When None (the contract path, until it threads the cluster — see coordination_notes),
4265	    fall back to the FLAT FIRST-WINS scan across all clusters (the prior behaviour).
4266	    """
4267	    if selected_cluster_id is not None:
4268	        _bdict = basket_by_cluster.get(selected_cluster_id) or {}
4269	        for _m in (_bdict.get("supporting_members") or []):
4270	            if str(_m.get("evidence_id") or "") == corroborator_eid:
4271	                return str(_m.get("direct_quote") or "")
4272	        # The corroborator is not a member of the SELECTED cluster — ungrounded for THIS
4273	        # selection (conservative). Do NOT fall through to a sibling cluster's span: that is
4274	        # exactly the cross-cluster mis-read this fix removes.
4275	        return ""
4276	    for _bdict in basket_by_cluster.values():
4277	        for _m in (_bdict.get("supporting_members") or []):
4278	            if str(_m.get("evidence_id") or "") == corroborator_eid:
4279	                _span = str(_m.get("direct_quote") or "")
4280	                if _span:
4281	                    return _span
4282	    return ""

4285	def corroborator_grounds_sentence_via_basket(
4286	    sentence_claim: str,
4287	    corroborator_eid: str,
4288	    basket_by_cluster: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
4289	    *,
4290	    selected_cluster_id: str | None = None,
4291	) -> bool:
4292	    """THE single (sentence, corroborator) grounding verdict, computed identically at every
4293	    render site (the legacy resolver append loop + its retention guard, AND the V30 contract
4294	    slot-regroup) so they can NEVER diverge.
4295	
4296	    I-beatboth-011 (#1289): bundles P1#2 (read the member's CLAIM-LOCAL ``direct_quote``, not
4297	    the broad evidence_pool row) with the ``corroborator_span_grounds_sentence`` predicate
4298	    (P1#4-corrected numeric paths). Wiring this ONE function at all three sites closes P1#1
4299	    (contract path was unfiltered) and P1#3 (the retention guard used unfiltered corroborators
4300	    and could strand a sentence). Faithfulness-TIGHTENING only — it removes UNSUPPORTED
4301	    attributions; the engine predicates are READ, never altered. Empty claim-local span =>
4302	    ungrounded => not attached (conservative), consistent across all sites.
4303	
4304	    I-beatboth-011 (#1289) P1 (multi-cluster span): ``selected_cluster_id`` threads the cluster
4305	    the corroborator was selected through to ``_claim_local_corroborator_span``, so a corroborator
4306	    that is a member of several clusters is grounded against the SELECTED cluster's span, not a
4307	    sibling's. None => the prior global first-match scan (the contract path lands here until it
4308	    threads the cluster — coordination_notes).
4309	    """
4310	    _span = _claim_local_corroborator_span(
4311	        corroborator_eid, basket_by_cluster, selected_cluster_id=selected_cluster_id
4312	    )
4313	    if not _span:
4314	        return False
4315	    if not corroborator_span_grounds_sentence(sentence_claim, _span):
4316	        return False
4317	    # I-deepfix-001 P2 (#1344): NLI co-support stacked BENEATH the lexical/numeric predicate
4318	    # (an AND, so it can ONLY tighten). Detach ONLY on a CONFIDENT all-clauses-non-entail
4319	    # verdict; a confirmed clause entailment keeps, an unavailable verdict keeps (degrade,
4320	    # never fights §-1.3 breadth on a model outage). Because all three corroborator sites
4321	    # (append loop, retention guard, contract slot-regroup) route through THIS function, the
4322	    # purity gate lands at a single source of truth. OFF (=0) => byte-identical.
4323	    if _citation_nli_purity_enabled():
4324	        _co = _corroborator_nli_co_supports(sentence_claim, _span)
4325	        if _co is False:
4326	            _PURITY_TELEMETRY["nli_detached"] += 1
4327	            return False
4328	        _PURITY_TELEMETRY["nli_kept" if _co is True else "nli_unknown_kept"] += 1
4329	    return True

4332	def _own_token_cited_span(
4333	    tok: "ProvenanceToken", evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]
4334	) -> str:
4335	    """The OWN token's cited sub-span of its evidence row's CURRENT ``direct_quote`` (falling
4336	    back to ``statement``). Empty when the row is missing or the (start,end) is out of bounds
4337	    on the current text — the I-deepfix-001 P2 (#1344) biblio-shows-cited-span input."""
4338	    ev = evidence_pool.get(tok.evidence_id)
4339	    if not ev:
4340	        return ""
4341	    dq = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
4342	    if tok.start < 0 or tok.end > len(dq) or tok.start >= tok.end:
4343	        return ""
4344	    return dq[tok.start:tok.end]

4347	def _own_token_span_reasserts(
4348	    tok: "ProvenanceToken",
4349	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
4350	    sentence_claim: str,
4351	) -> bool:
4352	    """I-deepfix-001 P2 (#1344): render-time DETERMINISTIC re-assert of an OWN cited token
4353	    against the CURRENT ``evidence_pool[eid].direct_quote`` — (a) the stored (start,end) is
4354	    still in bounds on the current span text, AND (b) the cited span still grounds the sentence
4355	    via the SAME lexical/numeric predicate strict_verify uses
4356	    (``corroborator_span_grounds_sentence``). This guards the I-wire-014 class where a
4357	    ``direct_quote`` was truncated / replaced between verify and render so the stored offsets no
4358	    longer point at grounding text. No model — pure bounds + overlap. Fail => withhold the
4359	    inline [N] (fail-closed), but the source STAYS in the bibliography and the min-retention
4360	    guard never lets the sentence go cited->uncited."""
4361	    ev = evidence_pool.get(tok.evidence_id)
4362	    if not ev:
4363	        return False
4364	    dq = ev.get("direct_quote") or ev.get("statement") or ""
4365	    if tok.start < 0 or tok.end > len(dq) or tok.start >= tok.end:
4366	        return False
4367	    return corroborator_span_grounds_sentence(sentence_claim, dq[tok.start:tok.end])

4370	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4371	# I-beatboth-003 (#1280): SURE-RAG per-citation relevance LABEL — demotion at the
4372	# render chokepoint. strict_verify (invariant #3) is relevance-BLIND: it passes a
4373	# source that shares two incidental words without establishing the required RELATION
4374	# (the off-topic-but-topical case). This labels each ALREADY-strict-verified citation
4375	# Supported / Insufficient / Refuted and demotes the Insufficient/Refuted ones from
4376	# the INLINE support set, with a HARD minimum-retention guard (never strand a sentence
4377	# uncited). It is a NEW dimension ADDED on top of strict_verify, NEVER a relaxation /
4378	# replacement of it, NEVER an input to is_verified or the six strict_verify checks, and
4379	# NEVER a hold/abstain — the report ALWAYS ships. Default-OFF (PG_RELEVANCE_GATE) =>
4380	# byte-identical legacy render. Single render chokepoint: BOTH production render paths
4381	# (the legacy resolver AND the V30 contract slot-regroup) funnel through
4382	# resolve_provenance_to_citations, so wiring it HERE covers both (no silent no-op).
4383	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4384	
4385	# Telemetry: per-process counters so a run can prove the gate FIRED (and how much it
4386	# demoted) in the output, not just that the flag was set (§-1.4 fired-not-configured).
4387	_RELEVANCE_TELEMETRY: dict[str, int] = {
4388	    "citations_judged": 0,
4389	    "labeled_supported": 0,
4390	    "labeled_insufficient": 0,
4391	    "labeled_refuted": 0,
4392	    "demoted_from_support": 0,        # cites actually removed from the inline support set
4393	    "retention_kept_weak": 0,         # demotion BLOCKED by the minimum-retention guard
4394	    "sentences_marked_weak": 0,       # sentences whose last support would have been demoted
4395	    "contradiction_flagged": 0,       # sentences that gained a refuted-contradiction flag
4396	    "judge_errors": 0,                # judge returned a SUPPORTED keep-fallback on a fault
4397	}

4417	def _classify_sentence_citations(
4418	    sv: "SentenceVerification",
4419	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
4420	    relevance_judge_fn,
4421	    corroborator_spans: dict[str, str] | None = None,
4422	) -> tuple[set[str], set[str], list[str], dict[str, str]]:
4423	    """Label each of a kept sentence's OWN cited tokens (Supported/Insufficient/Refuted)
4424	    and return ``(demote_eids, refute_eids, soft_warnings, labels)``.
4425	
4426	    iter-2 (Codex P1#1a): ``labels`` is the PERSISTED per-citation label side-output —
4427	    eid -> canonical label (SUPPORTED / INSUFFICIENT / REFUTED) for EVERY judged citation,
4428	    so the structured label survives onto the SentenceVerification (it was previously
4429	    computed implicitly and discarded). ``demote_eids`` is INSUFFICIENT-only and
4430	    ``refute_eids`` is REFUTED-only — two DISTINCT sets (Codex P1#1b), so Refuted is routed
4431	    to a contradiction flag, never merely folded into the demote set.
4432	
4433	    The CLAIM judged is the sentence's verifier-cleaned prose (citation artifacts stripped);
4434	    the SPAN is the token's cited sub-span of its evidence row's direct_quote — EXACTLY the
4435	    (claim, span) pair strict_verify already validated, so the relevance judge is the SAME
4436	    granularity. Only VALID tokens (evidence-id in pool, span in bounds) are judged; a token
4437	    strict_verify would have failed never reaches a kept sentence. Per-CITATION, not
4438	    per-sentence: an Insufficient/Refuted label removes ONLY that token's evidence_id from the
4439	    inline support set — the sentence and its remaining support citations are untouched (the
4440	    P0 crux: NEVER inherit the I-beatboth-001 'drop the whole sentence' option).
4441	
4442	    This function does NOT enforce minimum-retention — it only LABELS. The caller applies the
4443	    retention guard against the WHOLE sentence's surviving support set (including basket
4444	    corroborators), so a sentence is never stranded uncited.
4445	
4446	    ``corroborator_spans`` (eid -> span_text) carries the basket-corroborator INLINE citations
4447	    the resolver/contract-runner ADD to this sentence (members of the SAME claim basket the
4448	    generator did not cite directly). On the Gate-B contract path these are the DOMINANT inline
4449	    multi-citations (slot-fill emits one own-token per sentence), so the relevance gate MUST
4450	    judge them too or it is a near-no-op on the benchmark path. They are ADDITIVE citations:
4451	    demoting one can never strand the sentence (its own support remains + retention covers the
4452	    own-token case), so judging them is faithfulness-safe. SUPPORTS-membership was decided by
4453	    the SAME span-grounding check this gate calls relevance-blind, so they are exactly as
4454	    exposed as own tokens.
4455	    """
4456	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.relevance_judge import (  # noqa: PLC0415
4457	        LABEL_INSUFFICIENT,
4458	        LABEL_REFUTED,
4459	        LABEL_SUPPORTED,
4460	        judge_citation_relevance,
4461	    )
4462	
4463	    demote: set[str] = set()
4464	    refute: set[str] = set()
4465	    warnings: list[str] = []
4466	    labels: dict[str, str] = {}  # iter-2 P1#1a: persisted eid -> label for EVERY judged cite
4467	    claim = _verifier_cleaned_text(sv.sentence).strip()
4468	
4469	    def _judge_one(eid: str, span_text: str) -> None:
4470	        label, reason = judge_citation_relevance(
4471	            claim, span_text, relevance_judge_fn=relevance_judge_fn,
4472	        )
4473	        _RELEVANCE_TELEMETRY["citations_judged"] += 1
4474	        # iter-2 P1#1a: record the canonical label for THIS citation so the structured
4475	        # side-output lands on the SV (no longer computed-then-discarded). SUPPORTED kept
4476	        # citations are recorded too — an audit can see the full per-cite verdict map.
4477	        labels[eid] = label
4478	        if reason.startswith("judge_error:"):
4479	            _RELEVANCE_TELEMETRY["judge_errors"] += 1
4480	        if label == LABEL_SUPPORTED:
4481	            _RELEVANCE_TELEMETRY["labeled_supported"] += 1
4482	        elif label == LABEL_INSUFFICIENT:
4483	            _RELEVANCE_TELEMETRY["labeled_insufficient"] += 1
4484	            demote.add(eid)
4485	            warnings.append(f"{RELEVANCE_DEMOTED_PREFIX}:{eid}:{reason[:80]}")
4486	        elif label == LABEL_REFUTED:
4487	            _RELEVANCE_TELEMETRY["labeled_refuted"] += 1
4488	            refute.add(eid)
4489	            warnings.append(f"{RELEVANCE_REFUTED_PREFIX}:{eid}:{reason[:80]}")

4550	    baskets: list | None = None,

4601	        )

4608	    # rule). Built only when basket data is present, so OFF stays a no-op.

4610	    _carry_baskets = baskets is not None and cluster_id_by_evidence is not None

4613	            _ccid = str(getattr(_basket, "claim_cluster_id", "") or "")

4618	    #

4620	    # rows. B6/B8 additionally renders the WHOLE basket INLINE at the per-sentence
4621	    # citation: when a sentence cites a source that backs a claim basket, append the
4622	    # citation markers for ALL OTHER independently span-verified supporting members of

4694	            # ``statement[:300]`` — so the reader sees the exact text the [N] attributes, not a
4695	            # 300-char row prefix that may not contain the cited claim. OFF / no-span callers
