4869	                if e not in _demote_eids
4870	                and e not in _refute_eids
4871	                and corroborator_grounds_sentence_via_basket(
4872	                    _guard_claim_text, e, _basket_by_cluster, selected_cluster_id=_ccid
4873	                )
4874	            ]
4875	            # iter-2 P1#1a: PERSIST the structured per-citation LABEL map onto the SV. This is
4876	            # the JUDGE VERDICT for every judged cite (eid -> SUPPORTED/INSUFFICIENT/REFUTED) —
4877	            # orthogonal to the retention decision, so it is persisted UNCONDITIONALLY here
4878	            # (a retained-weak sentence still carries the honest INSUFFICIENT/REFUTED verdict
4879	            # in this map). Pre-fix this was COMPUTED then DISCARDED (the Codex P1 silent no-op):
4880	            # the labels never reached the verification record. Additive — never an input to
4881	            # is_verified.
4882	            if _rel_labels:
4883	                _merged_labels = dict(sv.relevance_labels or {})
4884	                _merged_labels.update(_rel_labels)
4885	                sv.relevance_labels = _merged_labels
4886	            if not _surviving_own and not _surviving_corro and _own_eids:
4887	                # Retention guard fires: un-demote (keep ALL own tokens as support), mark weak.
4888	                # iter-2 P1#3: ACTUALLY MARK THE STATEMENT WEAK — persist a
4889	                # ``relevance_statement_weak`` soft-warning on the SV (pre-fix only telemetry
4890	                # bumped; RELEVANCE_WEAK_PREFIX was defined but never constructed). The cite is
4891	                # KEPT (never stranded); the weak mark records that its last support did not
4892	                # establish the relation. Always-release: the sentence still ships.
4893	                #
4894	                # The ACTION soft-warnings (``relevance_demoted_insufficient`` /
4895	                # ``relevance_refuted_contradiction``) are DELIBERATELY NOT appended here: the
4896	                # retention guard un-demotes (the sets end EMPTY), so a "demoted"/"contradiction-
4897	                # flagged" warning would claim an action that did not happen — inconsistent with
4898	                # the (now empty) demote/refute sets. The honest verdict is still recorded in the
4899	                # ``relevance_labels`` map above; the only ACTION on this sentence is the weak
4900	                # mark. (Codex iter-2 internal-consistency: warnings must match the sets.)
4901	                _RELEVANCE_TELEMETRY["retention_kept_weak"] += len(_demote_eids) + len(_refute_eids)
4902	                _RELEVANCE_TELEMETRY["sentences_marked_weak"] += 1
4903	                _weak_eids = ",".join(sorted(_demote_eids | _refute_eids))
4904	                sv.soft_warnings = list(sv.soft_warnings) + [
4905	                    f"{RELEVANCE_WEAK_PREFIX}:{_weak_eids}"
4906	                ]
4907	                _demote_eids = set()
4908	                _refute_eids = set()
4909	            else:
4910	                # Demotion / contradiction-flag actually fires: PERSIST the human-readable
4911	                # demote/refute reason soft-warnings (``_rel_warnings``), which now MATCH the
4912	                # populated demote/refute sets below. (Moved into the else-branch per Codex
4913	                # iter-2: in the retention branch the sets are cleared, so these action warnings
4914	                # must NOT be present.)
4915	                if _rel_warnings:
4916	                    sv.soft_warnings = list(sv.soft_warnings) + list(_rel_warnings)
4917	                _RELEVANCE_TELEMETRY["demoted_from_support"] += len(_demote_eids) + len(_refute_eids)
4918	                if _refute_eids:
4919	                    _RELEVANCE_TELEMETRY["contradiction_flagged"] += 1
4920	            # CACHE the FINAL (post-retention) demote + refute sets on the SV so the V30 contract
4921	            # slot-regroup (contract_section_runner.py) drops the IDENTICAL eids without
4922	            # re-judging or re-deciding retention. The contract runner calls THIS resolve()
4923	            # with the SAME kept_sentences objects + SAME baskets, so the decision is valid for
4924	            # both render loops — one source of truth. Additive attributes; the OFF path never
4925	            # reaches here so they stay None (byte-identical). Carried by dataclasses.replace.
4926	            #
4927	            # iter-2 P1#1b + P1#2: Insufficient and Refuted are CACHED AS TWO DISTINCT SETS.
4928	            # ``relevance_demoted_eids`` is Insufficient-only (listed-not-load-bearing);
4929	            # ``relevance_refuted_eids`` is Refuted-only — the persisted CONTRADICTION-FLAG
4930	            # set. The contradiction is also recorded human-readably via the
4931	            # ``relevance_refuted_contradiction:<eid>:...`` soft-warning persisted above. Both
4932	            # render loops exclude the UNION of the two from inline support.
4933	            sv.relevance_demoted_eids = frozenset(_demote_eids)
4934	            sv.relevance_refuted_eids = frozenset(_refute_eids)
4935	
4936	        # Assign citation numbers only for surviving sentences. A demoted (Insufficient) or
4937	        # refuted token is EXCLUDED from the inline support set — it is NOT passed to _num_for,
4938	        # so it never becomes a numbered inline support cite (Insufficient => listed-not-load-
4939	        # bearing; Refuted => contradiction flag). On the OFF path both sets are empty so every
4940	        # token is included exactly as before (byte-identical).
4941	        used_nums: list[int] = []
4942	        # I-deepfix-001 P2 (#1344): render-time OWN-TOKEN bounds+overlap re-assert. Each own
4943	        # token already passed strict_verify UPSTREAM, but a ``direct_quote`` can be
4944	        # truncated / replaced between verify and render (I-wire-014), leaving the stored
4945	        # (start,end) pointing at non-grounding text. Re-assert against the CURRENT
4946	        # ``direct_quote``; a token that no longer grounds has its inline [N] WITHHELD
4947	        # (fail-closed) — but the source STAYS in the bibliography and the min-retention guard
4948	        # below never lets the sentence go cited->uncited. OFF (=0) => every token passes the
4949	        # (skipped) re-assert => byte-identical legacy render.
4950	        _purity_on = _citation_nli_purity_enabled()
4951	        _own_reassert_claim = _verifier_cleaned_text(sv.sentence)
4952	        _own_pass: list[ProvenanceToken] = []
4953	        _own_withheld: list[ProvenanceToken] = []
4954	        for tok in sv.tokens:
4955	            if tok.evidence_id in _demote_eids or tok.evidence_id in _refute_eids:
4956	                continue
4957	            if _purity_on and not _own_token_span_reasserts(
4958	                tok, evidence_pool, _own_reassert_claim
4959	            ):
4960	                _own_withheld.append(tok)
4961	            else:
4962	                _own_pass.append(tok)
4963	        # MINIMUM-RETENTION GUARD: never strand a sentence's own support. If EVERY surviving
4964	        # own token would be withheld by the re-assert, KEEP them all (the sentence keeps its
4965	        # own citations) — fail toward retention, consistent with the FORBIDDEN cited->uncited
4966	        # invariant. The withhold only fires when at least one own token still grounds.
4967	        if _purity_on and _own_withheld and not _own_pass:
4968	            _PURITY_TELEMETRY["own_token_retention_kept"] += len(_own_withheld)
4969	            _own_pass, _own_withheld = _own_withheld, []
4970	        for tok in _own_withheld:
4971	            _PURITY_TELEMETRY["withheld_own_token"] += 1
4972	            # Register the biblio row (source STAYS in the bibliography) but do NOT append the
4973	            # inline [N] for THIS sentence.
4974	            _num_for(tok.evidence_id, cited_span=_own_token_cited_span(tok, evidence_pool))
4975	        for tok in _own_pass:
4976	            _cs = _own_token_cited_span(tok, evidence_pool) if _purity_on else None
4977	            n = _num_for(tok.evidence_id, cited_span=_cs)
4978	            if n not in used_nums:
4979	                used_nums.append(n)
4980	        # I-arch-005 B6/B8 (#1257): INLINE multi-citation basket render. Append the
4981	        # citation markers for every OTHER independently span-verified (SUPPORTS) member
4982	        # of the basket(s) this sentence's OWN cited sources back, so a multi-source claim
4983	        # shows ALL its corroborating citations. The member's evidence_id is resolved into
4984	        # the SAME numbered bibliography via _num_for (which auto-enriches the new biblio
4985	        # row with the basket(s) the corroborator backs), and dedup'd against used_nums so
4986	        # the sentence's own citation is never doubled. Faithfulness: SUPPORTS-only (the
4987	        # verified_support_origin_count members), never the advisory clustered count;
4988	        # _verified_corroborators_for_tokens returns [] on the OFF path => byte-identical.
4989	        # I-beatboth-003: a corroborator that the relevance judge demoted/refuted for THIS
4990	        # sentence is likewise excluded from the inline support set (consistency with the
4991	        # per-token demotion above); OFF path => sets empty => byte-identical.
4992	        #
4993	        # I-beatboth-011 (#1289) — anti-mis-attribution corroborator filter (defect #6).
4994	        # A corroborator is attached by CLUSTER membership, not by whether its span grounds
4995	        # THIS sentence. Before appending its [N], require that its span actually carries the
4996	        # sentence's claim (>= MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP distinctive content words — the SAME
4997	        # predicate strict_verify uses for own tokens, READ here, never the engine). This
4998	        # removes UNSUPPORTED cross-claim attributions (e.g. [85] glued to a BLS sentence only
4999	        # [84] grounds) — faithfulness-TIGHTENING, never a relaxation. Own tokens (above) are
5000	        # NEVER filtered (already strict-verified). Genuine corroborators that share the claim's
5001	        # content words are KEPT, so real multi-citation (§-1.3) is preserved; only a member
5002	        # whose span does NOT carry this sentence's claim is dropped. The sentence ALWAYS
5003	        # retains its own tokens, so it is never stranded uncited.
5004	        # I-beatboth-011 P1#2: ground each corroborator against the basket member's CLAIM-LOCAL
5005	        # ``direct_quote`` (via corroborator_grounds_sentence_via_basket), NOT the broad
5006	        # evidence_pool row text — the SAME decision the retention guard (above) and the V30
5007	        # contract slot-regroup now use, so the three sites can never diverge.
5008	        # I-beatboth-011 P1 (multi-cluster span): iterate (eid, selected_cluster) pairs and
5009	        # ground each corroborator against the span of the cluster it was SELECTED through, so a
5010	        # corroborator that is a member of several clusters reads the RIGHT claim-local span.
5011	        _corro_claim_text = _verifier_cleaned_text(sv.sentence)
5012	        for _corro_eid, _corro_ccid in _verified_corroborators_with_clusters_for_tokens(
5013	            [tok.evidence_id for tok in sv.tokens]
5014	        ):
5015	            if _corro_eid in _demote_eids or _corro_eid in _refute_eids:
5016	                continue
5017	            if not corroborator_grounds_sentence_via_basket(
5018	                _corro_claim_text, _corro_eid, _basket_by_cluster,
5019	                selected_cluster_id=_corro_ccid,
5020	            ):
5021	                # This member's claim-local span does not carry THIS sentence's claim —
5022	                # attaching its [N] would be a wrong-claim citation (mis-attribution). Skip it.
5023	                # (It still appears in the bibliography as basket context; it is only withheld
5024	                # from inline support for this specific sentence.)
5025	                continue
5026	            n = _num_for(_corro_eid)
5027	            if n not in used_nums:
5028	                used_nums.append(n)
5029	        # I-deepfix-001 B9(c): collapse inline citation numbers that map to the SAME independent
5030	        # origin cluster (a scholarly mirror of one work) to ONE number + an "(also mirrored)" note,
5031	        # so "[11][12]" from one origin never reads as two independent corroborating sources. Distinct
5032	        # origins (real multi-source corroboration) are kept distinct (§-1.3). Faithfulness-neutral:
5033	        # every cited source still lists in the bibliography; only the inline double-count is removed.
5034	        # OFF / no-origin-data path returns used_nums unchanged => byte-identical legacy render.
5035	        _num_to_eid = {num: eid for eid, num in ev_to_num.items()}
5036	        _origin_by_num = {
5037	            num: _origin_by_eid.get(_num_to_eid.get(num, ""), "") for num in used_nums
5038	        }
5039	        used_nums, _mirrors_collapsed = collapse_mirror_citation_numbers(used_nums, _origin_by_num)
5040	        if _mirrors_collapsed:
5041	            _TOKEN_HONESTY_TELEMETRY["mirror_cites_collapsed"] = (
5042	                _TOKEN_HONESTY_TELEMETRY.get("mirror_cites_collapsed", 0) + _mirrors_collapsed
5043	            )
5044	        # Append citation markers (+ a render-honest "also mirrored" note when a same-origin mirror
5045	        # was folded, so the reader sees the corroboration was a mirror, not an independent source).
5046	        markers = "".join(f"[{n}]" for n in used_nums)
5047	        if _mirrors_collapsed:
5048	            markers += " (also mirrored)"
5049	        sentence_out = stripped + markers
5050	
5051	        # Gap-3: put Limitations sentences in a separate paragraph so
5052	        # they render on their own line in the final report.
5053	        if any("limitations_paragraph_pass_through" in w for w in sv.soft_warnings):
5054	            limitations_lines.append(sentence_out)
5055	        else:
5056	            findings_lines.append(sentence_out)
5057	
5058	    # F10 (I-arch-004 A3): the count of sentences ACTUALLY emitted into the
5059	    # rendered text — the honest post-resolve verified count the caller must
5060	    # report (NOT len(kept_sentences), which overstates because this loop drops
5061	    # degenerate fragments + F31 bogus-only sentences).
5062	    emitted_count = len(findings_lines) + len(limitations_lines)
5063	
5064	    findings_para = " ".join(findings_lines)
5065	    if limitations_lines:
5066	        limitations_para = " ".join(limitations_lines)
5067	        return (findings_para + "\n\n" + limitations_para, biblio, emitted_count)

content.text: That completes provenance_generator.py. No unprofessional names found — extremely well documented, systematic naming. Now let's look at the next file, quantified_analysis.py.

content.content:
1	"""I-meta-005 Phase 7 (#991) — Quantified analysis executor + binder (gap 9).
2	
3	Owns the **Execute** (run the deterministic script in the existing sandbox) and
4	**Bind** (attach one calc token per rendered computed number) stages of the
5	Extract -> Model -> Execute -> Bind -> Verify pipeline.
6	
7	``execute_quantified_model`` renders the validated spec to a FIXED Python script
8	(``tradeoff_modeler.render_script`` — no codegen LLM), runs it via the EXISTING
9	``code_executor.execute_analysis_script`` (sandbox unchanged), and pins each
10	computed field's canonical ``display_value`` (via the ONE shared
11	``_canonical_display``) so Regime C verification is an exact-string equality plus
12	a numeric backstop, and so the spec replays deterministically (gap-19).
13	
14	The ``QuantifiedResult`` it returns is intentionally self-describing with plain
15	data (no tradeoff_modeler dataclasses leak into the verifier): each field carries
16	``value`` / ``display_value`` / ``modeled_used`` (names that must be labeled
17	"(modeled assumption)") / ``sourced_tokens`` (the evidence spans Regime C returns
18	so resolve_provenance_to_citations cites the inputs).
19	
20	SPEND-FREE: deterministic render + sandbox execution of FIXED Python; no network.
21	"""
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import ast
25	import json
26	import logging
27	import os
28	import re
29	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
30	from typing import Any
31	
32	from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import (
33	    ModelSpec,
34	    _canonical_display,
35	    output_referenced_inputs,
36	)
37	from src.polaris_graph.tools.code_executor import execute_analysis_script
38	
39	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
40	
41	# ``{{calc:<field>}}`` placeholder the Writer/section template emits where a
42	# computed number should be rendered + bound.
43	_CALC_PLACEHOLDER_RE = re.compile(r"\{\{calc:(?P<field>[^}]+)\}\}")
44	
45	# Two extracted datapoints describing the SAME quantity (same label + unit) whose
46	# values disagree by more than this are a sourced-input conflict — surfaced in
47	# manifest telemetry so the operator sees the corpus contradiction (named, Law VI).
48	_CALC_CONFLICT_REL_TOL = float(os.environ.get("PG_CALC_CONFLICT_REL_TOL", "0.05"))
49	
50	# I-pipe-012 (#1237): typed-status discrimination for the quantified differentiator.
51	# The pre-fix function returned a bare ``None`` at three distinct death points
52	# (spec_provider raised / returned non-dict / build_quantified_spec rejected /
53	# execution failed / no verified sentences) that a caller could only tell apart by
54	# scraping the free-text log. ``telem["quantified_status"]`` is the discrete, machine-
55	# readable verdict so a post-run audit can separate "no numbers to model" (decline)
56	# from "the differentiator silently broke" (transport/parse failure). The success path
57	# stamps ``ok``. None of these statuses changes a VERIFIED claim — they only label WHY
58	# the (already strict_verify/Regime-C-gated) section did or did not land.
59	QUANTIFIED_STATUS_OK = "ok"                       # spec validated, executed, ≥1 sentence survived Regime C
60	QUANTIFIED_STATUS_DECLINED_NO_SPEC = "declined_no_spec"  # Writer returned no dict (decline OR collapsed transport — see cross-file)
61	QUANTIFIED_STATUS_EMPTY_TRANSPORT = "empty_transport"    # reserved: a true empty-200/404 transport miss (only reachable once the caller stops collapsing it into None — see cross_file_deferred)
62	QUANTIFIED_STATUS_PARSE_ERROR = "parse_error"     # spec_provider RAISED, or emitted a dict that failed hard validation / execution
63	
64	# Kill-switch (LAW VI): default-ON typed-status + bounded transient retry. Set to
65	# "0"/"false" to REVERT to the pre-fix behavior — a bare ``None`` return with NO
66	# ``quantified_status`` key added to telem and a SINGLE spec_provider call (no retry).
67	_TYPED_STATUS_ENABLED = os.environ.get("PG_QUANTIFIED_TYPED_STATUS", "1").strip().lower() not in (
68	    "0", "false", "no", "off",
69	)
70	
71	# Bounded retries for a TRANSIENT spec_provider transport/parse failure (the RAISED
72	# path only — a non-dict return is a Writer DECLINE, never retried; re-billing a
73	# decline is waste). Total attempts = 1 + retries. Default 1 retry (2 attempts).
74	_SPEC_PROVIDER_RETRIES = max(0, int(os.environ.get("PG_QUANTIFIED_SPEC_RETRIES", "1")))
75	
76	# FIX-2 (#1344) — quantified filler suppression (LAW VI kill-switch, default-ON).
77	# Withholds invented unit-free scalar OUTPUTS that merely relate >=2 distinct sourced
78	# inputs (e.g. "displacement productivity ratio 2.14286", "restructuring efficiency 5",
79	# "net job shift 1" — dimensionless numbers built by dividing unrelated percentages from
80	# different studies x a free scaling_factor). FLAG/withhold-not-faithfulness: a dimensioned
81	# result (currency/percent/count, or a transform of a single sourced number) passes through,
82	# nothing in the corpus/bibliography is dropped, and the faithfulness engine is untouched.
83	# Set PG_QUANTIFIED_FILLER_SUPPRESS=0 to revert to byte-identical legacy rendering.
84	_FILLER_SUPPRESS_ENABLED = os.environ.get(
85	    "PG_QUANTIFIED_FILLER_SUPPRESS", "1"
86	).strip().lower() not in ("0", "false", "no", "off")
87	_DIMENSIONLESS_DISPLAY_KINDS = frozenset({"number", "ratio"})
88	_FILLER_MIN_SOURCED_INPUTS = max(
89	    2, int(os.environ.get("PG_QUANTIFIED_FILLER_MIN_SOURCED", "2"))
90	)
91	
92	
93	# U27 (#1344) — Writer-shortlist curation (LAW VI kill-switch, default-ON).
94	# The quantified-spec Writer (the ONLY billed step) previously received the FIRST
95	# N extracted datapoints in raw evidence-pool iteration order (`sourced[:N]` in the
96	# run_honest_sweep_r3.py `_q_spec_provider` closure). On real large clinical corpora
97	# the LEADING datapoints are scrape chrome / binary garbage — PDF object offsets
98	# ("%PDF- 13 0 obj"), base64 auth blobs, CDN image dimensions ("382x200px" parsed as
99	# 204669%), and phone numbers ("tel:080 4669 4311") — so the Writer was handed an
100	# incoherent, absurd-valued set and correctly returned {"model_id":"none"} (->
101	# no_spec_returned), even though many clean modelable clinical numbers (2.5%
102	# infections, carbidopa bioavailability 99%, levodopa AUC +55%, half-life 1.5h) sat
103	# buried deeper in the list. This curates the shortlist so clean, well-labeled,
104	# plausibly-valued datapoints reach the Writer instead of raw iteration-order junk.
105	#
106	# INPUT HYGIENE, NOT a faithfulness filter (faithfulness_risk: neutral): the FULL
107	# extracted pool still flows to build_quantified_spec for datapoint matching, no
108	# source is dropped, and every downstream gate (strict_verify / Regime C / provenance)
109	# is untouched. Set PG_QUANTIFIED_SHORTLIST_CLEAN=0 to REVERT to byte-identical
110	# ``sourced[:N]`` (no curation, no added telemetry).
111	_SHORTLIST_CLEAN_ENABLED = os.environ.get(
112	    "PG_QUANTIFIED_SHORTLIST_CLEAN", "1"
113	).strip().lower() not in ("0", "false", "no", "off")
114	
115	# Absolute ceiling on a plausible percentage magnitude. Junk CDN dims / phone numbers
116	# parse as 5-6 digit "percentages" (204669%, 82972%, 206921%); real clinical/economic
117	# percentages sit far below this generous cap (the clean drb_78 max is 99%). LAW VI
118	# env-tunable — a CAP, not a target; it only ever REMOVES a garbage candidate.
119	_SHORTLIST_MAX_PCT = float(os.environ.get("PG_QUANTIFIED_SHORTLIST_MAX_PCT", "1000"))
120	
121	# Substrings whose presence in a datapoint's label OR context marks it as scrape
122	# chrome / binary / markup garbage rather than a modelable clinical/economic number:
123	# URL schemes, markdown link/image syntax, tel:/mailto: links, PDF-binary markers,
124	# HTML entities, CDN crop params, and the Unicode replacement char (binary noise).
125	_SHORTLIST_JUNK_MARKERS = (
126	    "http://", "https://", "www.", "](", "![", "tel:", "mailto:",
127	    "%pdf", "endobj", "endstream", "/bbox", "/length", "xref",
128	    "&", "&#", "crop=fp", "fp_zoom", "cdn-assets", "�",
129	)
130	# I-deepfix-001 U27 iter2 (Codex): the bare substrings " obj" and "stream" were REMOVED — they
131	# false-positived on ordinary clinical prose (" objective", "bloodstream") and would wrongly drop
132	# valid numbers, falsely triggering `no_modelable_numbers`. PDF binary junk is now caught by the
133	# UNAMBIGUOUS keywords above (endobj/endstream/%pdf/xref//length//bbox) plus a PRECISE PDF
134	# object-header regex ("13 0 obj") below — none of which occur in clinical/economic prose.
135	_SHORTLIST_PDF_OBJ_RE = re.compile(r"\b\d+\s+\d+\s+obj\b")
136	# A long unbroken alphanumeric run = base64 auth blob / hash / accession, never prose
137	# (English words / clinical labels break well under this length).
138	_SHORTLIST_BASE64_RE = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z0-9+/]{24,}")
139	
140	
141	def is_junk_modelable_datapoint(datapoint: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
142	    """True iff ``datapoint`` is scrape-chrome / binary / markup garbage that must
143	    NOT be offered to the quantified-spec Writer (U27, #1344).
144	
145	    Deterministic, offline, faithfulness-neutral: this only decides whether a
146	    candidate NUMBER is coherent enough to put in front of the (billed) spec LLM. It
147	    NEVER drops a source and NEVER touches a verification gate — the full extracted
148	    pool still flows to ``build_quantified_spec`` for datapoint matching. When the
149	    kill-switch is OFF it is never consulted (see ``select_writer_candidate_numbers``).
150	
151	    A datapoint is junk when ANY of: (1) it has no label (nothing to reason over);
152	    (2) its label or context contains a URL / markdown-link / image / tel / PDF-binary
153	    / HTML-entity / CDN chrome marker; (3) its label or context contains a long
154	    unbroken alphanumeric run (base64 blob / hash / accession); (4) its unit is a
155	    percentage but the value exceeds the plausible-percentage ceiling.
156	    """
157	    label = str(datapoint.get("label") or "")
158	    context = str(datapoint.get("context") or "")
159	    hay = f"{label}\n{context}".lower()
160	
161	    # (1) empty label — no describable quantity for the Writer.
162	    if not label.strip():
163	        return True
164	    # (2) URL / markdown-link / image / tel / PDF-binary / HTML-entity / CDN chrome.
165	    for marker in _SHORTLIST_JUNK_MARKERS:
166	        if marker in hay:
167	            return True
168	    # PDF object header ("13 0 obj") — precise, does not match "objective"/other prose.
169	    if _SHORTLIST_PDF_OBJ_RE.search(hay):
170	        return True
171	    # (3) base64 auth blob / hash / accession (a long unbroken alphanumeric run).
172	    if _SHORTLIST_BASE64_RE.search(label) or _SHORTLIST_BASE64_RE.search(context):
173	        return True
174	    # (4) an absurd percentage magnitude (CDN dims / phone numbers parsed as %); a
175	    #     non-numeric "value" under a % unit is itself garbage.
176	    unit = str(datapoint.get("unit") or "").strip()
177	    if unit in ("%", "percent"):
178	        try:
179	            if abs(float(datapoint.get("value"))) > _SHORTLIST_MAX_PCT:
180	                return True
181	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
182	            return True
183	    return False
184	
185	
186	def select_writer_candidate_numbers(
187	    datapoints: list[dict[str, Any]], *, limit: int | None = None,
188	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
189	    """Curate the quantified-spec Writer shortlist (U27, #1344).
190	
191	    Returns the first ``limit`` CLEAN datapoints (junk dropped by
192	    ``is_junk_modelable_datapoint``), preserving the input order so the result is
193	    deterministic (same input -> same output, byte-for-byte). ``limit=None`` returns
194	    every clean datapoint.
195	
196	    With ``PG_QUANTIFIED_SHORTLIST_CLEAN=0`` (kill-switch OFF) this is byte-identical
197	    to ``datapoints[:limit]`` — the legacy unranked/unfiltered slice — so a revert is
198	    a pure no-op. Faithfulness-neutral: this only orders/filters the LLM's INPUT
199	    shortlist; the full pool is still used downstream for datapoint matching.
200	    """
201	    if not _SHORTLIST_CLEAN_ENABLED:
202	        return datapoints[:limit] if limit is not None else list(datapoints)
203	    clean = [dp for dp in datapoints if not is_junk_modelable_datapoint(dp)]
204	    return clean[:limit] if limit is not None else clean
205	
206	
207	def is_low_value_filler_output(field: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
208	    """True iff ``field`` is a unit-free number/ratio scalar relating >=2 distinct
209	    sourced inputs — the invented-filler signature. Counts sourced INPUTS (not ev_ids),
210	    so "3% - 2%" from the SAME source is still caught. A dimensioned result, a non-
211	    number/ratio display kind, or a scalar over <2 sourced inputs is NOT filler."""
212	    if not _FILLER_SUPPRESS_ENABLED:
213	        return False
214	    if str(field.get("unit") or "").strip():
215	        return False
216	    if str(field.get("display_kind") or "number") not in _DIMENSIONLESS_DISPLAY_KINDS:
217	        return False
218	    n_sourced = sum(
219	        1 for t in (field.get("sourced_tokens") or []) if isinstance(t, dict)
220	    )
221	    return n_sourced >= _FILLER_MIN_SOURCED_INPUTS
222	
223	
224	# ── FINDING #4 (I-deepfix-001 tail B3, #1344) — unit-compatibility + cited-operand
225	#    gate for composed quantified quantities (LAW VI kill-switch, default ON) ──────
226	# THE DEFECT (drb_72 forensic rank 4): the modeler emitted
227	#   "The wage to employment impact ratio is 2.1.[4]"
228	# — a pipeline division ``0.42% / 0.2 percentage-points`` rendered with a PRIMARY
229	# citation as if the source stated it. Two independent faults:
230	#   (b) it divides a PERCENT by a PERCENTAGE-POINT — incompatible units in the SAME
231	#       dimension (both proportions) — so the resulting dimensionless "ratio" is
232	#       dimensionally MEANINGLESS; and
233	#   (a) it is a computed number attributed to a source that never states it.
234	# The FIX-2 filler suppressor above only withholds unit-FREE scalars over >=2 sourced
235	# inputs; it does NOT check unit COMPATIBILITY (a percent-/currency-typed output that
236	# divides %/pp escapes it) and does not gate a ratio that mixes a cited number with an
237	# uncited (modeled) assumption. This gate is the precise dimensional-validity +
238	# cited-operand check the finding asks for.
239	#
240	# FAITHFULNESS (LAW §-1.3): a RENDER-ONLY withhold at the composition seam (same
241	# category as FIX-2). A withheld output stays in the spec + quantified_model.json; NO
242	# source, corpus row, basket, or verified claim is dropped; strict_verify / Regime C /
243	# NLI / the 4-role D8 engine are untouched. It never adds a cap/target/thinner — it only
244	# refuses to RENDER a dimensionally-invalid or non-cited computed number. Set
245	# ``PG_QUANTIFIED_UNIT_COMPAT=0`` to REVERT byte-identically.
246	_UNIT_COMPAT_ENABLED = os.environ.get(
247	    "PG_QUANTIFIED_UNIT_COMPAT", "1"
248	).strip().lower() not in ("0", "false", "no", "off")
249	
250	# A dimensionless output (empty unit) declared as one of these kinds is a "ratio" in the
251	# plain sense — its operands' units are supposed to CANCEL. Dividing incompatible units
252	# and calling the result dimensionless is exactly the finding's category error.
253	_RATIO_DISPLAY_KINDS = frozenset({"number", "ratio"})
254	
255	# Unit classes that share a DIMENSION but must NOT be divided into a dimensionless ratio
256	# (their ratio is semantically meaningless). Members of the SAME family divided by each
257	# other are ratio-incompatible; DIFFERENT families divided (e.g. currency / time = a
258	# legitimate rate) are ALLOWED. Extend via config, never a hardcoded per-run target.
259	_UNIT_FAMILY = {"percent": "proportion", "percentage_point": "proportion"}
260	
261	
262	def _unit_class(unit: Any) -> str | None:
263	    """Normalize a raw unit string to a coarse unit CLASS, or ``None`` for a neutral /
264	    dimensionless / unknown unit (an empty unit, or one we do not recognize — treated as
265	    neutral so it never triggers a false incompatibility). PURE, offline."""
266	    u = str(unit or "").strip().lower().rstrip(". ")
267	    if not u:
268	        return None
269	    if u in {"%", "percent", "percentage", "percentages", "pct", "per cent"}:
270	        return "percent"
271	    if u in {
272	        "pp", "ppt", "ppts", "pps", "pctpt", "pct pt", "pct pts",
273	        "percentage point", "percentage points",
274	        "percentage-point", "percentage-points",
275	    }:
276	        return "percentage_point"
277	    if u in {"$", "usd", "us$", "dollar", "dollars"}:
278	        return "currency_usd"
279	    if u in {"€", "eur", "euro", "euros"}:
280	        return "currency_eur"
281	    if u in {"£", "gbp", "pound", "pounds"}:
282	        return "currency_gbp"
283	    return u
284	
285	
286	def _ratio_incompatible(a: str | None, b: str | None) -> bool:
287	    """True iff dividing a class-``a`` operand by a class-``b`` operand yields a
288	    dimensionally-meaningless ratio: two DIFFERENT members of the SAME unit family
289	    (e.g. percent / percentage-point). Neutral (None) operands never trigger it."""
290	    if a is None or b is None or a == b:
291	        return False
292	    fam_a = _UNIT_FAMILY.get(a)
293	    return fam_a is not None and fam_a == _UNIT_FAMILY.get(b)
294	
295	
296	def formula_units_incompatible(formula: str, unit_class_by_name: dict[str, str | None]) -> bool:
297	    """True iff ``formula`` combines incompatible units anywhere in its AST:
298	
299	    * an ``Add`` / ``Sub`` of two DIFFERENT non-neutral unit classes (adding across
300	      classes is never valid — you cannot add dollars to years), OR
301	    * a ``Div`` of two DIFFERENT members of the SAME unit family (a meaningless
302	      same-dimension ratio, e.g. percent / percentage-point).
303	
304	    Multiplication and cross-family division (a legitimate rate, e.g. currency / time)
305	    are ALLOWED. PURE + offline; an unparseable formula is treated as NOT-incompatible
306	    (fail-open — never a false drop on a formula we cannot analyze)."""
307	    try:
308	        tree = ast.parse(str(formula or ""), mode="eval")
309	    except SyntaxError:
310	        return False
311	    bad = False
312	
313	    def _classes(node: ast.AST) -> set[str]:
314	        """Return the set of non-neutral unit classes among ``node``'s leaves; set the
315	        enclosing ``bad`` flag on any incompatible combination."""
316	        nonlocal bad
317	        if isinstance(node, ast.Expression):
318	            return _classes(node.body)
319	        if isinstance(node, ast.Name):
320	            c = unit_class_by_name.get(node.id)
321	            return {c} if c is not None else set()
322	        if isinstance(node, ast.Constant):
323	            return set()
324	        if isinstance(node, ast.UnaryOp):
325	            return _classes(node.operand)
326	        if isinstance(node, ast.BinOp):
327	            left = _classes(node.left)
328	            right = _classes(node.right)
329	            op = node.op
330	            if isinstance(op, (ast.Add, ast.Sub)):
331	                combined = left | right
332	                if len({c for c in combined if c is not None}) >= 2:
333	                    bad = True
334	                return combined
335	            if isinstance(op, ast.Div):
336	                if len(left) == 1 and len(right) == 1:
337	                    (lc,) = tuple(left)
338	                    (rc,) = tuple(right)
339	                    if _ratio_incompatible(lc, rc):
340	                        bad = True
341	                    if lc == rc:
342	                        return set()  # same class -> units cancel -> dimensionless
343	                return left | right
344	            # Mult / Pow / Mod / FloorDiv -> product/other; allowed to mix classes.
345	            return left | right
346	        if isinstance(node, ast.Call):
347	            out: set[str] = set()
348	            for a in node.args:
349	                out |= _classes(a)
350	            # abs/min/max/round preserve the operand class; other funcs (sqrt/log/exp/…)
351	            # take a dimensionless argument -> a dimensionless result.
352	            if getattr(node.func, "id", "") in {"abs", "min", "max", "round"}:
353	                return out
354	            return set()
355	        return set()
356	
357	    _classes(tree)
358	    return bad
359	
360	
361	def _leaf_unit_classes(
362	    node: ast.AST, unit_class_by_name: dict[str, str | None]
363	) -> set[str]:
364	    """Set of the NON-NEUTRAL unit classes among ``node``'s operand leaves (a neutral /
365	    unknown / dimensionless leaf — an unrecognized unit or a bare constant — contributes
366	    nothing). PURE + offline. Used to test whether a division's numerator and denominator
367	    each resolve to one shared, cancelling unit class."""
368	    if isinstance(node, ast.Expression):
369	        return _leaf_unit_classes(node.body, unit_class_by_name)
370	    if isinstance(node, ast.Name):
371	        c = unit_class_by_name.get(node.id)
372	        return {c} if c is not None else set()
373	    if isinstance(node, ast.UnaryOp):
374	        return _leaf_unit_classes(node.operand, unit_class_by_name)
375	    if isinstance(node, ast.BinOp):
376	        return (
377	            _leaf_unit_classes(node.left, unit_class_by_name)
378	            | _leaf_unit_classes(node.right, unit_class_by_name)
379	        )
380	    if isinstance(node, ast.Call):
381	        out: set[str] = set()
382	        for a in node.args:
383	            out |= _leaf_unit_classes(a, unit_class_by_name)
384	        return out
385	    return set()
386	
387	
388	def _dimension_preserving_only(node: ast.AST) -> bool:
389	    """True iff ``node`` combines its leaves using ONLY dimension-PRESERVING operations
390	    (Add / Sub / unary +/- / abs / round / min / max) — no Mult / Div / Pow / Mod /
391	    FloorDiv, which would compound or cancel dimensions. So the node's overall dimension
392	    equals its single leaf unit class, which is precisely what makes a division of two
393	    such nodes a genuine units-cancel ratio. PURE + offline."""
394	    if isinstance(node, ast.Expression):
395	        return _dimension_preserving_only(node.body)
396	    if isinstance(node, (ast.Name, ast.Constant)):
397	        return True
398	    if isinstance(node, ast.UnaryOp):
399	        return _dimension_preserving_only(node.operand)
400	    if isinstance(node, ast.BinOp):
401	        if not isinstance(node.op, (ast.Add, ast.Sub)):
402	            return False
403	        return (
404	            _dimension_preserving_only(node.left)
405	            and _dimension_preserving_only(node.right)
406	        )
407	    if isinstance(node, ast.Call):
408	        if getattr(node.func, "id", "") not in {"abs", "min", "max", "round"}:
409	            return False
410	        return all(_dimension_preserving_only(a) for a in node.args)
411	    return False
412	
413	
414	def formula_is_same_unit_cancelling_ratio(
415	    formula: str, unit_class_by_name: dict[str, str | None]
416	) -> bool:
417	    """True iff ``formula``'s dimensionless value is PRODUCED by a genuine same-unit
418	    cancelling division: the top-level operation is a ``Div`` whose numerator and
419	    denominator EACH resolve to the SAME single NON-NEUTRAL unit class (so the units
420	    cancel to dimensionless — e.g. ``a / b`` over two USD amounts).
421	
422	    A SUM / DIFFERENCE / PRODUCT (``a + b`` / ``a - b`` / ``a * b``), a division whose
423	    two operands do NOT share exactly one non-neutral class (``percent / percentage-point``
424	    or ``currency / years``), or a division whose operands themselves compound dimensions
425	    (contain Mult / Div / Pow, e.g. ``a * b / c``) does NOT qualify. An unparseable
426	    formula is NOT a cancelling ratio (fail-CLOSED for the exemption — defer to the FIX-2
427	    filler suppressor). PURE + offline; mirrors ``formula_units_incompatible``'s AST view."""
428	    try:
429	        tree = ast.parse(str(formula or ""), mode="eval")
430	    except SyntaxError:
431	        return False
432	    node: ast.AST = tree.body if isinstance(tree, ast.Expression) else tree
433	    # Peel dimension-preserving unary/abs/round wrappers around the top division.
434	    while True:
435	        if isinstance(node, ast.UnaryOp):
436	            node = node.operand
437	            continue
438	        if (
439	            isinstance(node, ast.Call)
440	            and getattr(node.func, "id", "") in {"abs", "round"}
441	            and node.args
442	        ):
443	            node = node.args[0]
444	            continue
445	        break
446	    if not (isinstance(node, ast.BinOp) and isinstance(node.op, ast.Div)):
447	        return False
448	    if not (
449	        _dimension_preserving_only(node.left)
450	        and _dimension_preserving_only(node.right)
451	    ):
452	        return False
453	    left = _leaf_unit_classes(node.left, unit_class_by_name)
454	    right = _leaf_unit_classes(node.right, unit_class_by_name)
455	    if len(left) != 1 or len(right) != 1:
456	        return False
457	    (lc,) = tuple(left)
458	    (rc,) = tuple(right)
459	    return lc is not None and lc == rc
460	
461	
462	def should_withhold_composed_output(output_field: Any, spec: Any) -> bool:
463	    """FINDING #4 composition-time drop decision for a single declared ``OutputField``.
464	
465	    Returns True to WITHHOLD the output's rendered sentence when EITHER:
466	      (b) its formula combines incompatible units (``formula_units_incompatible``), OR
467	      (a) it is a dimensionless RATIO (empty unit, number/ratio kind) that MIXES a cited
468	          (sourced) operand with an uncited (modeled/assumed) operand — presenting an
469	          assumption-laden ratio as if the source stated it.
470	
471	    Kill-switch OFF (``PG_QUANTIFIED_UNIT_COMPAT=0``) => always False (byte-identical).
472	    PURE except for the kill-switch read; no faithfulness gate is touched."""
473	    if not _UNIT_COMPAT_ENABLED:
474	        return False
475	    unit_class_by_name: dict[str, str | None] = {}
476	    kind_by_name: dict[str, str] = {}
477	    for s in spec.sourced_inputs:
478	        unit_class_by_name[s.name] = _unit_class(s.unit)
479	        kind_by_name[s.name] = "sourced"
480	    for m in spec.modeled_inputs:
481	        unit_class_by_name[m.name] = _unit_class(m.unit)
482	        kind_by_name[m.name] = "modeled"
483	
484	    # (b) unit-incompatibility anywhere in the formula.
485	    if formula_units_incompatible(getattr(output_field, "formula", ""), unit_class_by_name):
486	        logger.info(
487	            "[quantified_analysis] FINDING#4 withhold %r: unit-incompatible formula %r",
488	            getattr(output_field, "name", "?"), getattr(output_field, "formula", ""),
489	        )
490	        return True
491	
492	    # (a) a dimensionless ratio mixing a cited operand with an uncited (modeled) one.
493	    is_dimensionless_ratio = (
494	        not str(getattr(output_field, "unit", "") or "").strip()
495	        and str(getattr(output_field, "display_kind", "number") or "number")
496	        in _RATIO_DISPLAY_KINDS
497	    )
498	    if is_dimensionless_ratio:
499	        refs = output_referenced_inputs(spec, getattr(output_field, "name", ""))
500	        ref_kinds = {kind_by_name.get(r) for r in refs}
501	        if "sourced" in ref_kinds and "modeled" in ref_kinds:
502	            logger.info(
503	                "[quantified_analysis] FINDING#4 withhold %r: dimensionless ratio mixes "
504	                "a cited (sourced) operand with an uncited (modeled) assumption",
505	                getattr(output_field, "name", "?"),
506	            )
507	            return True
508	    return False
509	
510	
511	def is_valid_cited_ratio(output_field: Any, spec: Any) -> bool:
512	    """FINDING #4 iter-2 (#1344 tail B3): True iff ``output_field`` is a dimensionally-
513	    VALID, fully-CITED dimensionless ratio that the unit-compat gate has AFFIRMATIVELY
514	    cleared — a same-unit (units-cancel) ratio over sourced operands with NO modeled
515	    (uncited) operand.
516	
517	    THE ITER-2 P1 FIX: such a quantity is legitimate and MUST render under production
518	    defaults. The older coarse FIX-2 filler-suppressor (``is_low_value_filler_output``)
519	    blanks EVERY unit-free number/ratio over >=2 sourced inputs and would wrongly blank a
520	    valid same-unit (e.g. USD/USD) cited ratio; the caller uses this predicate to let the
521	    unit-compat gate GOVERN that path so FIX-2 never blanks a valid cited ratio.
522	
523	    Returns False (defers entirely to the legacy FIX-2 behaviour — byte-identical) when:
524	    the unit-compat gate is OFF; FINDING #4 would WITHHOLD the output (unit-mismatch or a
525	    cited+modeled mix); the output is not a dimensionless number/ratio; it references no
526	    cited (sourced) operand OR any modeled operand; OR — the ITER-3 P1 fix — its formula is
527	    not a GENUINE same-unit cancelling division (a dimensionless-declared SUM / DIFFERENCE /
528	    PRODUCT over cited operands is NOT a units-cancel ratio). PURE except for the kill-switch
529	    read; touches no faithfulness gate."""
530	    if not _UNIT_COMPAT_ENABLED:
531	        return False
532	    is_dimensionless_ratio = (
533	        not str(getattr(output_field, "unit", "") or "").strip()
534	        and str(getattr(output_field, "display_kind", "number") or "number")
535	        in _RATIO_DISPLAY_KINDS
536	    )
537	    if not is_dimensionless_ratio:
538	        return False
539	    # The unit-compat gate must have CLEARED it (compatible units, not a cited+modeled mix).
540	    if should_withhold_composed_output(output_field, spec):
541	        return False
542	    refs = output_referenced_inputs(spec, getattr(output_field, "name", ""))
543	    sourced_names = {s.name for s in spec.sourced_inputs}
544	    modeled_names = {m.name for m in spec.modeled_inputs}
545	    refs_sourced = any(r in sourced_names for r in refs)
546	    refs_modeled = any(r in modeled_names for r in refs)
547	    if not (refs_sourced and not refs_modeled):
548	        return False
549	    # ITER-3 P1 (Codex): the exemption from the FIX-2 filler suppressor is ONLY for a
550	    # GENUINE same-unit cancelling ratio — the formula's dimensionless value must arise
551	    # from a Div of two operands sharing one non-neutral unit class (units cancel). Without
552	    # this, ANY dimensionless-declared output over cited-only operands escaped FIX-2, so a
553	    # plain SUM ``a + b`` of two cited USD amounts (empty unit, display_kind="number") was
554	    # wrongly exempted and rendered — reopening the FIX-2 filler hole for non-ratio
555	    # dimensionless multi-source fillers. A sum / difference / product does NOT qualify.
556	    unit_class_by_name: dict[str, str | None] = {}
557	    for s in spec.sourced_inputs:
558	        unit_class_by_name[s.name] = _unit_class(s.unit)
559	    for m in spec.modeled_inputs:
560	        unit_class_by_name[m.name] = _unit_class(m.unit)
561	    return formula_is_same_unit_cancelling_ratio(
562	        getattr(output_field, "formula", ""), unit_class_by_name
563	    )
564	
565	
566	def detect_sourced_conflicts(
567	    sourced_numbers: list[dict[str, Any]],
568	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
569	    """Flag datapoints that describe the SAME quantity (same label+unit) but whose
570	    values disagree by > ``PG_CALC_CONFLICT_REL_TOL``. Telemetry only — does not
571	    gate. Returns a list of conflict records {label, unit, values, rel_spread}."""
572	    groups: dict[tuple[str, str], list[float]] = {}
573	    for dp in sourced_numbers:
574	        label = str(dp.get("label", "")).strip().lower()
575	        unit = str(dp.get("unit", "")).strip().lower()
576	        if not label:
577	            continue
578	        try:
579	            val = float(dp.get("value"))
580	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
581	            continue
582	        groups.setdefault((label, unit), []).append(val)
583	
584	    conflicts: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
585	    for (label, unit), vals in groups.items():
586	        if len(vals) < 2:
587	            continue
588	        lo, hi = min(vals), max(vals)
589	        denom = max(abs(lo), abs(hi), 1e-9)
590	        rel_spread = abs(hi - lo) / denom
591	        if rel_spread > _CALC_CONFLICT_REL_TOL:
592	            conflicts.append({
593	                "label": label, "unit": unit,
594	                "values": sorted(set(vals)), "rel_spread": rel_spread,
595	            })
596	    return conflicts
597	
598	
599	@dataclass
600	class QuantifiedResult:
601	    model_id: str
602	    spec_hash: str
603	    spec: ModelSpec
604	    script: str
605	    # field_id -> {value, display_value, modeled_used: [names],
606	    #             sourced_tokens: [{ev_id,start,end,raw}]}
607	    fields: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=dict)
608	
609	    def key(self) -> tuple[str, str]:
610	        return (self.model_id, self.spec_hash)
611	
612	    def display_value(self, field_id: str) -> str | None:
613	        f = self.fields.get(field_id)
614	        return None if f is None else f.get("display_value")
615	
616	    def calc_token(self, field_id: str) -> str:
617	        return f"[#calc:{self.model_id}:{self.spec_hash}:{field_id}]"
618	
619	
620	def _modeled_set(spec: ModelSpec) -> set[str]:
621	    return {m.name for m in spec.modeled_inputs}
622	
623	
624	def _sourced_tokens_for(spec: ModelSpec, refs: set[str]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
625	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
626	    for s in spec.sourced_inputs:
627	        if s.name in refs:
628	            out.append({
629	                "ev_id": s.ev_id,
630	                "start": int(s.literal_start),
631	                "end": int(s.literal_end),
632	                "raw": s.raw_literal,
633	            })
634	    return out
635	
636	
637	def _output_for_field(field_id: str) -> str:
638	    """Map a field id to its underlying output name.
639	
640	    output            -> "tco"
641	    sensitivity point -> "tco@discount=0.06"  -> "tco"
642	    break-even        -> "tco.break_even"     -> "tco"
643	    """
644	    base = field_id.split("@", 1)[0]
645	    if base.endswith(".break_even"):
646	        base = base[: -len(".break_even")]
647	    return base
648	
649	
650	async def execute_quantified_model(
651	    spec: ModelSpec,
652	    evidence_rows: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
653	    *,
654	    run_dir: str | None = None,
655	    timeout: int | None = None,
656	) -> QuantifiedResult | None:
657	    """Render -> execute (sandbox) -> pin per-field display_value -> persist.
658	
659	    Returns a ``QuantifiedResult`` or ``None`` if execution fails (fail-closed:
660	    a model that does not execute cleanly contributes no verified numbers).
661	    """
662	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import render_script
663	
664	    script = render_script(spec)
665	    exec_result = await execute_analysis_script(script, input_data=None, timeout=timeout)
666	    if not exec_result.get("success") or not isinstance(exec_result.get("result"), dict):
667	        logger.warning(
668	            "[quantified_analysis] execute failed for model %s: %s",
669	            spec.model_id, str(exec_result.get("error"))[:160],
670	        )
671	        return None
672	
673	    parsed = exec_result["result"]
674	    raw_outputs = parsed.get("outputs") or {}
675	    raw_sens = parsed.get("sensitivity") or {}
676	    raw_be = parsed.get("break_even") or {}
677	    modeled_names = _modeled_set(spec)
678	
679	    fields: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
680	
681	    def _add_output_field(field_id: str, value: Any, *, exclude: str | None = None):
682	        out_name = _output_for_field(field_id)
683	        out = spec.output_by_name(out_name)
684	        if out is None:
685	            return
686	        try:
687	            fval = float(value)
688	        except (TypeError, ValueError):
689	            return
690	        is_break_even = field_id.endswith(".break_even")
691	        # break-even renders the solve-var VALUE (a threshold), not the output's
692	        # currency/percent — display it as a plain number.
693	        if is_break_even:
694	            disp_unit, disp_kind = "", "number"
695	        else:
696	            disp_unit, disp_kind = out.unit, out.display_kind
697	        display = _canonical_display(fval, disp_unit, disp_kind)
698	        refs = output_referenced_inputs(spec, out_name)
699	        modeled_used = sorted((refs & modeled_names) - ({exclude} if exclude else set()))
700	        fields[field_id] = {

701	            "value": fval,
702	            "display_value": display,
703	            # display_kind + unit let Regime C RE-CANONICALIZE the adjacent number
704	            # (Codex diff-gate P1-1/P1-2): the parsed adjacent value must format to
705	            # EXACTLY display_value — no suffix-match, no magnitude-scaled drift.
706	            "display_kind": disp_kind,
707	            "unit": disp_unit,
708	            "modeled_used": modeled_used,
709	            "sourced_tokens": _sourced_tokens_for(spec, refs),
710	        }
711	
712	    for name, value in raw_outputs.items():
713	        _add_output_field(str(name), value)
714	    for fid, value in raw_sens.items():
715	        # "tco@discount=0.06" -> the swept var is stated as the point, exclude it
716	        swept = None
717	        if "@" in fid:
718	            after = fid.split("@", 1)[1]
719	            swept = after.split("=", 1)[0]
720	        _add_output_field(str(fid), value, exclude=swept)
721	    for fid, value in raw_be.items():
722	        out_name = _output_for_field(str(fid))
723	        var = spec.solve_for.var if spec.solve_for else None
724	        _add_output_field(str(fid), value, exclude=var)
725	
726	    result = QuantifiedResult(
727	        model_id=spec.model_id, spec_hash=spec.spec_hash, spec=spec,
728	        script=script, fields=fields,
729	    )
730	
731	    if run_dir:
732	        try:
733	            _persist(result, run_dir)
734	        except OSError as exc:
735	            logger.warning("[quantified_analysis] persist failed: %s", str(exc)[:160])
736	
737	    return result
738	
739	
740	def _persist(result: QuantifiedResult, run_dir: str) -> str:
741	    """Write the audit/replay bundle (gap-19): spec + rendered script + per-field
742	    canonical display values + spec_hash."""
743	    spec = result.spec
744	    bundle = {
745	        "model_id": result.model_id,
746	        "spec_hash": result.spec_hash,
747	        "title": spec.title,
748	        "script": result.script,
749	        "sourced_inputs": [
750	            {"name": s.name, "value": s.value, "unit": s.unit, "ev_id": s.ev_id,
751	             "raw_literal": s.raw_literal, "literal_span": [s.literal_start, s.literal_end]}
752	            for s in spec.sourced_inputs
753	        ],
754	        "modeled_inputs": [
755	            {"name": m.name, "base": m.base, "unit": m.unit,
756	             "sweep": [m.sweep_lo, m.sweep_hi, m.sweep_step]}
757	            for m in spec.modeled_inputs
758	        ],
759	        "outputs": [
760	            {"name": o.name, "unit": o.unit, "display_kind": o.display_kind,
761	             "formula": o.formula}
762	            for o in spec.outputs
763	        ],
764	        "fields": {
765	            fid: {
766	                "value": f["value"], "display_value": f["display_value"],
767	                "display_kind": f.get("display_kind"), "unit": f.get("unit"),
768	                # Codex diff-gate P2-1: persist the full per-field audit record
769	                # (modeled inputs that must be labeled + the source-input spans).
770	                "modeled_used": f.get("modeled_used", []),
771	                "sourced_tokens": f.get("sourced_tokens", []),
772	            }
773	            for fid, f in result.fields.items()
774	        },
775	    }
776	    path = os.path.join(run_dir, "quantified_model.json")
777	    with open(path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
778	        json.dump(bundle, fh, indent=2, sort_keys=True)
779	    return path
780	
781	
782	def bind_calc_tokens(prose: str, result: QuantifiedResult) -> str:
783	    """Replace each ``{{calc:<field>}}`` placeholder with the field's canonical
784	    display value IMMEDIATELY followed by its calc token, so the verifier binds
785	    the token to the exact rendered number (token adjacency, brief §1.4)."""
786	    def _sub(m: re.Match) -> str:
787	        fid = m.group("field")
788	        disp = result.display_value(fid)
789	        if disp is None:
790	            return m.group(0)  # leave unknown placeholder untouched (will fail verify)
791	        return f"{disp}{result.calc_token(fid)}"
792	
793	    return _CALC_PLACEHOLDER_RE.sub(_sub, prose)
794	
795	
796	def render_decision_matrix_prose(spec: ModelSpec, result: QuantifiedResult) -> str:
797	    """Deterministically template a one-sentence-per-number trade-off paragraph
798	    with ``{{calc:<field>}}`` placeholders + per-sentence "(modeled assumption)"
799	    labels where the field uses a modeled input. NOT an LLM call — the NUMBERS
800	    come from the verified executor; only the connective prose is templated, so
801	    no model can confabulate a computed value. One calc number per sentence
802	    (sentence-level keep/drop, brief §1.5)."""
803	    def _label(field_id: str) -> str:
804	        f = result.fields.get(field_id) or {}
805	        return (" " + _MODELED_LABEL_TEXT) if f.get("modeled_used") else ""
806	
807	    sents: list[str] = []
808	    for o in spec.outputs:
809	        if o.name in result.fields:
810	            # FINDING #4 (#1344 tail B3) — the unit-compat gate is AUTHORITATIVE for the
811	            # ratio path and runs FIRST: withhold a unit-incompatible ratio (e.g. a
812	            # percent divided by a percentage-point) or a dimensionless ratio that mixes
813	            # a cited operand with an uncited (modeled) assumption — a dimensionally-
814	            # meaningless / non-cited computed number attributed to a source.
815	            if should_withhold_composed_output(o, spec):
816	                continue
817	            # FIX-2 (#1344): withhold unit-free scalar outputs that merely relate >=2
818	            # sourced inputs (invented "ratio"/"number" filler). Break-even / sensitivity
819	            # render on their own exempt branch below, so they are never suppressed here.
820	            # ITER-2 P1: a same-unit, fully-cited ratio that FINDING #4 has AFFIRMATIVELY
821	            # cleared (``is_valid_cited_ratio``) is a valid quantity and MUST render — the
822	            # coarse FIX-2 filler-suppressor must NOT blank it. Only outputs FINDING #4 did
823	            # not clear as a valid cited ratio are still subject to the FIX-2 suppressor.
824	            if not is_valid_cited_ratio(o, spec) and is_low_value_filler_output(
825	                result.fields[o.name]
826	            ):
827	                continue
828	            human = o.name.replace("_", " ")
829	            sents.append(f"The {human} is {{{{calc:{o.name}}}}}{_label(o.name)}.")
830	    if spec.solve_for is not None:
831	        be = f"{spec.solve_for.output}.break_even"
832	        if be in result.fields:
833	            var = spec.solve_for.var.replace("_", " ")
834	            sents.append(f"The break-even {var} is {{{{calc:{be}}}}}{_label(be)}.")
835	    return " ".join(sents)
836	
837	
838	_MODELED_LABEL_TEXT = "(modeled assumption)"
839	
840	
841	def _stamp_status(telem: dict[str, Any], status: str) -> None:
842	    """I-pipe-012 (#1237): ADD the discrete typed ``quantified_status`` to telem,
843	    but ONLY when the kill-switch is ON. With ``PG_QUANTIFIED_TYPED_STATUS=0`` this
844	    is a no-op so the returned telem dict is byte-identical to the pre-fix shape
845	    (existing ``firing_status`` + every other key is preserved either way — this
846	    only ever ADDS a key, never mutates or removes an existing one)."""
847	    if _TYPED_STATUS_ENABLED:
848	        telem["quantified_status"] = status
849	
850	
851	async def _call_spec_provider_with_retry(
852	    spec_provider, question: str, sourced_numbers: list[dict[str, Any]],
853	) -> tuple[Any, BaseException | None]:
854	    """I-pipe-012 (#1237): call the (billed) ``spec_provider`` with a BOUNDED retry
855	    on a TRANSIENT transport/parse failure (the RAISED path only).
856	
857	    Returns ``(raw_spec, last_exc)``. On success ``raw_spec`` is the provider's
858	    return value (which may legitimately be a non-dict DECLINE) and ``last_exc`` is
859	    None. If every attempt raised, ``raw_spec`` is None and ``last_exc`` is the final
860	    exception. A non-dict return is NOT retried here (that is a Writer decline, not a
861	    transient fault — re-billing it is waste); only a raised exception is retried.
862	
863	    When the kill-switch is OFF, exactly ONE attempt is made (no retry), preserving
864	    the pre-fix single-call billing profile. NEVER fabricates a spec on failure."""
865	    attempts = (1 + _SPEC_PROVIDER_RETRIES) if _TYPED_STATUS_ENABLED else 1
866	    last_exc: BaseException | None = None
867	    for attempt in range(attempts):
868	        try:
869	            raw_spec = await spec_provider(question, sourced_numbers)
870	            return raw_spec, None
871	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — transient transport/parse fault; bounded retry
872	            last_exc = exc
873	            if attempt + 1 < attempts:
874	                logger.warning(
875	                    "[quantified_analysis] spec_provider raised (attempt %d/%d), retrying: %s",
876	                    attempt + 1, attempts, str(exc)[:160],
877	                )
878	    return None, last_exc
879	
880	
881	async def run_quantified_section(
882	    question: str,
883	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
884	    *,
885	    spec_provider,
886	    run_dir: str | None = None,
887	    credibility_analysis: Any = None,  # I-cred-008b (#1162): advisory per-claim disclosure; None => byte-identical
888	) -> tuple[str | None, dict[str, Any]]:
889	    """Sweep-facing orchestrator: Extract -> Model -> Execute -> Bind ->
890	    Verify(Regime C) -> verified "Quantified Trade-off" section.
891	
892	    ``spec_provider`` is an ASYNC callable ``(question, sourced_numbers) ->
893	    raw_spec_dict | None`` (the Writer in prod; a fake in tests) — the ONLY
894	    billed step, isolated here so the sync validation in ``build_quantified_spec``
895	    and the deterministic execute/verify stay spend-free-testable.
896	
897	    Returns ``(section_markdown | None, telemetry)``. The section is produced
898	    ONLY when a spec validates, executes, and at least one computed sentence
899	    survives Regime C.
900	    """
901	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (
902	        resolve_provenance_to_citations,
903	        strict_verify,
904	    )
905	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.tradeoff_modeler import build_quantified_spec
906	    from src.polaris_graph.tools.evidence_extractor import (
907	        extract_numbers_from_evidence,
908	    )
909	
910	    # FIX D-2 (#1182): ``firing_status`` is the LOUD, single-field reason the
911	    # quantified differentiator did or did not land — set at EVERY return point so a
912	    # ``spec_produced=False`` / ``fired=False`` manifest is never silent about WHERE it
913	    # died. The consumer ``quantified_degradation_disclosure`` (run_honest_sweep_r3.py)
914	    # already reads ``telemetry.get("firing_status")`` for its reader-facing disclosure,
915	    # but this producer never populated it — so a no-op surfaced only as a buried log.
916	    # Distinct values let a post-run audit tell "broken" (spec_provider_error /
917	    # execution_failed) apart from "legitimately inapplicable" (no_spec_returned with a
918	    # genuine Writer decline) without reading the raw log. Default = the pre-spec state.
919	    telem: dict[str, Any] = {
920	        "enabled": True, "spec_produced": False, "execution_success": False,
921	        "outputs": 0, "sourced_inputs": 0, "modeled_inputs": 0,
922	        "verified_sentences": 0, "dropped_sentences": 0, "conflicts": [],
923	        "firing_status": "started",
924	    }
925	
926	    sourced_numbers = extract_numbers_from_evidence(evidence_pool)
927	    telem["conflicts"] = detect_sourced_conflicts(sourced_numbers)
928	    telem["sourced_numbers_extracted"] = len(sourced_numbers)
929	
930	    # U27 (#1344): curate the Writer shortlist so clean, plausibly-valued datapoints
931	    # reach the (billed) spec LLM instead of raw iteration-order chrome/binary junk.
932	    # INPUT HYGIENE only — the FULL sourced pool still flows to build_quantified_spec
933	    # for datapoint matching below, and every verification gate is untouched. The
934	    # closure that actually shortlists to the Writer (run_honest_sweep_r3.py) applies
935	    # the SAME deterministic select_writer_candidate_numbers, so this telemetry mirrors
936	    # what the Writer will see. Kill-switch (PG_QUANTIFIED_SHORTLIST_CLEAN=0) => no
937	    # curation, no added telem keys, no short-circuit => byte-identical to the pre-fix path.
938	    if _SHORTLIST_CLEAN_ENABLED:
939	        _writer_candidates = select_writer_candidate_numbers(sourced_numbers)
940	        telem["writer_candidates"] = len(_writer_candidates)
941	        telem["sourced_numbers_junk"] = len(sourced_numbers) - len(_writer_candidates)
942	        # HONEST DISCLOSURE, not a silent no-op: if numbers WERE extracted but EVERY one
943	        # is scrape-chrome/binary garbage, there is a genuine data-shape reason the Writer
944	        # cannot model. Surface it as a DISTINCT status ("no_modelable_numbers") and skip
945	        # the wasted billed call, rather than letting the Writer decline on junk into the
946	        # ambiguous "no_spec_returned". Classed HONEST-EMPTY (declined_no_spec typed
947	        # status) — the differentiator did not silently break; the corpus simply carried
948	        # no clean modelable number. When at least one clean candidate survives, we DO
949	        # proceed to the Writer (this is the U27 fix: clean numbers now reach the LLM).
950	        if sourced_numbers and not _writer_candidates:
951	            telem["firing_status"] = "no_modelable_numbers"
952	            _stamp_status(telem, QUANTIFIED_STATUS_DECLINED_NO_SPEC)
953	            logger.warning(
954	                "[quantified_analysis] NO-OP (no_modelable_numbers): %d numbers were "
955	                "extracted but ALL were scrape-chrome/binary junk after shortlist "
956	                "curation (no clean modelable datapoint to offer the Writer)",
957	                len(sourced_numbers),
958	            )
959	            return None, telem
960	
961	    # I-pipe-012 (#1237): bounded retry on a TRANSIENT raised transport/parse fault
962	    # (kill-switch ON). With PG_QUANTIFIED_TYPED_STATUS=0 this is a single call (no
963	    # retry) and the except-branch below is byte-identical to the pre-fix path.
964	    raw_spec, _spec_exc = await _call_spec_provider_with_retry(
965	        spec_provider, question, sourced_numbers,
966	    )
967	    if _spec_exc is not None:
968	        # UNAMBIGUOUSLY broken: the Writer/transport raised (e.g. a 404 on the
969	        # generator route surfaced as an exception). Loud, distinct, non-aborting.
970	        telem["firing_status"] = "spec_provider_error"
971	        telem["firing_error"] = str(_spec_exc)[:200]
972	        # I-pipe-012 (#1237): a raised provider is a transport/parse fault (we already
973	        # exhausted the bounded retry) — the discrete "silently broke" verdict.
974	        _stamp_status(telem, QUANTIFIED_STATUS_PARSE_ERROR)
975	        logger.warning(
976	            "[quantified_analysis] NO-OP (spec_provider_error): spec_provider raised: %s",
977	            str(_spec_exc)[:160],
978	        )
979	        return None, telem
980	    if not isinstance(raw_spec, dict):
981	        # AMBIGUOUS by construction: a prod ``spec_provider`` (the Writer closure in
982	        # run_honest_sweep_r3.py) collapses BOTH a transport failure (404 / empty 200 →
983	        # no JSON parsed) AND a legitimate Writer decline ({"model_id":"none"}) into a
984	        # bare ``None`` before it reaches us. We CANNOT tell broken from inapplicable
985	        # here — the caller lane must stop collapsing the two (see module summary). Tag
986	        # it honestly rather than guess a confident reason we can't support.
987	        telem["firing_status"] = "no_spec_returned"
988	        # I-pipe-012 (#1237): a non-dict return is the Writer DECLINE (or a caller-
989	        # collapsed transport miss — see cross-file note). We map it to
990	        # declined_no_spec because that is all we can HONESTLY assert module-side; the
991	        # true empty_transport status only becomes reachable once the caller stops
992	        # collapsing a 404/empty-200 into the same bare None (cross_file_deferred).
993	        _stamp_status(telem, QUANTIFIED_STATUS_DECLINED_NO_SPEC)
994	        logger.warning(
995	            "[quantified_analysis] NO-OP (no_spec_returned): spec_provider returned "
996	            "no dict (transport failure OR legitimate Writer decline — caller collapses "
997	            "both into None; %d sourced numbers were available)",
998	            len(sourced_numbers),
999	        )
1000	        return None, telem
1001	
1002	    spec = build_quantified_spec(
1003	        question, sourced_numbers, evidence_pool,
1004	        spec_llm=lambda _q, _s: raw_spec,
1005	        # I-fix-001: capture the EXACT fail-closed gate into telemetry so the silent
1006	        # no-op names itself in the DURABLE manifest. Sweep run.log captures stdout,
1007	        # not the stderr WARNINGs build_quantified_spec emits — so before this the
1008	        # cert-run spec_validation_rejected had no attributable gate. Additive: the
1009	        # key is written only when a reason fires (None spec_llm => byte-identical).
1010	        on_reject=lambda _r: telem.__setitem__("spec_reject_reason", _r),
1011	    )
1012	    if spec is None:
1013	        # The Writer returned a dict but it FAILED hard validation in
1014	        # build_quantified_spec (datapoint identity, formula AST, material-dependency,
1015	        # etc.). A defensible-but-rejected model, not a transport failure.
1016	        telem["firing_status"] = "spec_validation_rejected"
1017	        # I-pipe-012 (#1237): the Writer emitted a dict that FAILED hard validation —
1018	        # a malformed/unparseable spec, classed with the parse_error family (the
1019	        # differentiator broke on a bad payload, not a clean decline).
1020	        _stamp_status(telem, QUANTIFIED_STATUS_PARSE_ERROR)
1021	        logger.warning(
1022	            "[quantified_analysis] NO-OP (spec_validation_rejected): Writer emitted a "
1023	            "spec dict but it failed build_quantified_spec validation (fail-closed): %s",
1024	            telem.get("spec_reject_reason", "unattributed"),
1025	        )
1026	        return None, telem
1027	    telem["spec_produced"] = True
1028	    telem["model_id"] = spec.model_id
1029	    telem["sourced_inputs"] = len(spec.sourced_inputs)
1030	    telem["modeled_inputs"] = len(spec.modeled_inputs)
1031	
1032	    result = await execute_quantified_model(spec, evidence_pool, run_dir=run_dir)
1033	    if result is None:
1034	        telem["firing_status"] = "execution_failed"
1035	        # I-pipe-012 (#1237): the sandbox returned no clean outputs — a parse/execution
1036	        # fault on an otherwise-valid spec, classed with the parse_error family.
1037	        _stamp_status(telem, QUANTIFIED_STATUS_PARSE_ERROR)
1038	        logger.warning(
1039	            "[quantified_analysis] NO-OP (execution_failed): spec validated but the "
1040	            "deterministic sandbox execution did not return clean outputs (model %s)",
1041	            spec.model_id,
1042	        )
1043	        return None, telem
1044	    telem["execution_success"] = True
1045	    telem["outputs"] = len(spec.outputs)
1046	
1047	    prose = render_decision_matrix_prose(spec, result)
1048	    bound = bind_calc_tokens(prose, result)
1049	    # FIX-2 (#1344): if every computed output was suppressed as low-value unit-free
1050	    # filler (and there is no break-even/sensitivity sentence), the bound prose is empty.
1051	    # Withhold the section under a DISTINCT honest status rather than letting it fall
1052	    # through to the Regime-C ``no_verified_sentences`` path (which would mislabel an
1053	    # intentional filler-withhold as a faithfulness failure).
1054	    if not bound.strip():
1055	        telem["firing_status"] = "suppressed_low_value_quantified"
1056	        telem["quantified_filler_suppressed"] = True
1057	        _stamp_status(telem, QUANTIFIED_STATUS_DECLINED_NO_SPEC)
1058	        logger.warning(
1059	            "[quantified_analysis] NO-OP (suppressed_low_value_quantified): all computed "
1060	            "outputs were unit-free filler scalars relating >=2 sourced inputs (model %s)",
1061	            spec.model_id,
1062	        )
1063	        return None, telem
1064	    report = strict_verify(
1065	        bound, evidence_pool, quantified_models={result.key(): result},
1066	    )
1067	    telem["verified_sentences"] = report.total_kept
1068	    telem["dropped_sentences"] = report.total_dropped
1069	    if report.total_kept == 0:
1070	        # The spec executed but EVERY computed sentence failed Regime C (e.g. an
1071	        # unlabeled modeled assumption, a number≠display mismatch). No verified prose.
1072	        telem["firing_status"] = "no_verified_sentences"
1073	        # I-pipe-012 (#1237): the spec executed but EVERY computed sentence failed
1074	        # Regime C — this is the faithfulness gate doing its job (a legitimate,
1075	        # NON-broken decline-to-emit). Class it as declined_no_spec (the
1076	        # differentiator did not silently break; it correctly emitted nothing).
1077	        _stamp_status(telem, QUANTIFIED_STATUS_DECLINED_NO_SPEC)
1078	        logger.warning(
1079	            "[quantified_analysis] NO-OP (no_verified_sentences): %d computed "
1080	            "sentence(s) all failed Regime C verification (model %s)",
1081	            report.total_dropped, spec.model_id,
1082	        )
1083	        return None, telem
1084	
1085	    # I-cred-008b (#1162) SITE 4/4 (quantified trade-off): populate the advisory per-claim disclosure
1086	    # on the kept SVs BEFORE resolve. This path returns (section_md, telem) — it has NO SectionResult,
1087	    # so the populated SVs do NOT flow through kept_sentences_pre_resolve. To SURFACE them, we emit the
1088	    # per-claim disclosure rows in `telem["claim_disclosure"]` (the runner merges them into
1089	    # claim_disclosure.json). None => byte-identical (no populate, no telem key).
1090	    if credibility_analysis is not None:
1091	        from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.credibility_pass import apply_disclosure_to_svs
1092	        report.kept_sentences = apply_disclosure_to_svs(
1093	            report.kept_sentences, credibility_analysis,
1094	        )
1095	        telem["claim_disclosure"] = [
1096	            {
1097	                "sentence": getattr(_sv, "sentence", ""),
1098	                "span_verdict": getattr(_sv, "span_verdict", ""),
1099	                "credibility_weight": getattr(_sv, "credibility_weight", None),
1100	                "independent_origin_count": getattr(_sv, "independent_origin_count", None),
1101	                "certainty_label": getattr(_sv, "certainty_label", ""),
1102	                "soft_warnings": list(getattr(_sv, "soft_warnings", None) or []),
1103	            }
1104	            for _sv in report.kept_sentences
1105	        ]
1106	
1107	    # Reached only when a spec validated, executed, and ≥1 computed sentence survived
1108	    # Regime C — the differentiator actually FIRED.
1109	    telem["firing_status"] = "fired"
1110	    # I-pipe-012 (#1237): the success verdict — a parseable spec produced a verified
1111	    # payload (the kept sentences, surfaced via verified_sentences / the rendered
1112	    # section). "ok+payload" per the acceptance criteria.
1113	    _stamp_status(telem, QUANTIFIED_STATUS_OK)
1114	
1115	    rendered, _biblio = resolve_provenance_to_citations(
1116	        report.kept_sentences, evidence_pool,
1117	    )
1118	    # I-deepfix-001 F3 (#1344): surface the section-LOCAL bibliography so the caller can
1119	    # remap this section's local ``[N]`` markers onto the GLOBAL multi-section bibliography
1120	    # (the quantified section is assembled outside the multi-section pipeline, so its local
1121	    # [1][2] otherwise collide with global [1]=Acemoglu/[2]=Autor and its input sources never
1122	    # appear in References). Additive telemetry field; byte-identical when unused.
1123	    telem["section_biblio"] = _biblio
1124	    section_md = f"### Quantified Trade-off\n\n{rendered}"
